PHQ-9, GAD-7, BP, Fall Risk, Med Adherence 13 14 15 @assessments_bp.route("/start") 16 @assessments_bp.route("/start/") 17 @login_required 18 def start(visit_id=None): 19 template = get_or_create_default_template(db.session) 20 sections = template.questions 21 22 # Check for existing draft 23 draft = AssessmentDraft.query.filter_by( 24 patient_id=current_user.id, 25 visit_id=visit_id, 26 template_id=template.id, 27 ).first() 28 29 current_step = draft.current_step if draft else 0 30 saved_answers = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) if draft else {} 31 request_token = str(uuid.uuid4()) 32 33 return render_template( 34 "assessments/wizard.html", 35 template=template, 36 sections=sections, 37 section=sections[current_step], 38 step=current_step, 39 current_step=current_step, 40 total_steps=TOTAL_STEPS, 41 visit_id=visit_id, 42 saved_answers=saved_answers, 43 request_token=request_token, 44 ) 45 46 47 @assessments_bp.route("/step/", methods=["POST"]) 48 @login_required 49 def wizard_step(step): 50 template = get_or_create_default_template(db.session) 51 sections = template.questions 52 visit_id = request.form.get("visit_id", type=int) 53 54 # Collect all answers from form 55 answers = {} 56 for key, val in request.form.items(): 57 if key not in ("csrf_token", "visit_id", "request_token", "step"): 58 answers[key] = val 59 60 # Save draft 61 draft = AssessmentDraft.query.filter_by( 62 patient_id=current_user.id, 63 visit_id=visit_id, 64 template_id=template.id, 65 ).first() 66 67 if draft: 68 existing = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) 69 existing.update(answers) 70 draft.partial_answers_json = json.dumps(existing) 71 draft.current_step = step 72 else: 73 draft = AssessmentDraft( 74 patient_id=current_user.id, 75 visit_id=visit_id, 76 template_id=template.id, 77 partial_answers_json=json.dumps(answers), 78 current_step=step, 79 ) 80 db.session.add(draft) 81 db.session.commit() 82 83 saved_answers = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) 84 request_token = request.form.get("request_token", str(uuid.uuid4())) 85 86 if step >= TOTAL_STEPS: 87 # Show review page 88 return render_template( 89 "assessments/_review.html", 90 template=template, 91 sections=sections, 92 answers=saved_answers, 93 visit_id=visit_id, 94 request_token=request_token, 95 ) 96 97 return render_template( 98 "assessments/_step.html", 99 template=template, 100 section=sections[step], 101 step=step, 102 total_steps=TOTAL_STEPS, 103 visit_id=visit_id, 104 saved_answers=saved_answers, 105 request_token=request_token, 106 ) 107 108 109 @assessments_bp.route("/submit", methods=["POST"]) 110 @login_required 111 def submit(): 112 template = get_or_create_default_template(db.session) 113 visit_id = request.form.get("visit_id", type=int) 114 request_token = request.form.get("request_token", "") 115 116 # Idempotency check 117 if request_token: 118 existing = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(request_token=request_token).first() 119 if existing: 120 return redirect(url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=existing.id)) 121 122 # Get answers from draft 123 draft = AssessmentDraft.query.filter_by( 124 patient_id=current_user.id, 125 visit_id=visit_id, 126 template_id=template.id, 127 ).first() 128 129 if not draft: 130 flash("No assessment data found. Please start again.", "danger") 131 return redirect(url_for("assessments.start", visit_id=visit_id)) 132 133 answers = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) 134 scores = calculate_scores(answers) 135 risk_level, explanations = calculate_risk_level(scores) 136 137 result = AssessmentResult( 138 patient_id=current_user.id, 139 visit_id=visit_id, 140 template_id=template.id, 141 template_version=template.version, 142 answers_json=json.dumps(answers), 143 scores_json=json.dumps(scores), 144 risk_level=risk_level, 145 explanation_snapshot_json=json.dumps(explanations), 146 request_token=request_token or None, 147 ) 148 db.session.add(result) 149 db.session.delete(draft) 150 db.session.commit() 151 152 if request.headers.get("HX-Request"): 153 resp = jsonify({"redirect": url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=result.id)}) 154 resp.headers["HX-Redirect"] = url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=result.id) 155 return resp 156 return redirect(url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=result.id)) 157 158 159 @assessments_bp.route("/result/") 160 @login_required 161 def result(assessment_id): 162 assessment = db.session.get(AssessmentResult, assessment_id) 163 if not assessment: 164 flash("Assessment not found.", "danger") 165 return redirect(url_for("assessments.history")) 166 167 # 1	from flask import Blueprint, render_template, request, flash, redirect, url_for, jsonify
2	from app.extensions import db
3	from app.models.coverage import CoverageZone, ZoneAssignment, ZoneDeliveryWindow
4	from app.models.scheduling import Clinician
5	from app.utils.auth import role_required
6	from app.utils.audit import log_action
7	
8	coverage_bp = Blueprint("coverage", __name__, url_prefix="/coverage")
9	
10	
11	@coverage_bp.route("/zones")
12	@role_required("administrator")
13	def zones():
14	    all_zones = CoverageZone.query.order_by(CoverageZone.name).all()
15	    return render_template("coverage/zones.html", zones=all_zones)
16	
17	
18	@coverage_bp.route("/zones", methods=["POST"])
19	@role_required("administrator")
20	def create_zone():
21	    name = request.form.get("name", "").strip()
22	    description = request.form.get("description", "").strip()
23	    zip_codes_raw = request.form.get("zip_codes", "").strip()
24	
25	    if not name:
26	        flash("Zone name is required.", "danger")
27	        return redirect(url_for("coverage.zones"))
28	
29	    distance_band_min = request.form.get("distance_band_min", 0, type=float)
30	    distance_band_max = request.form.get("distance_band_max", 0, type=float)
31	    min_order_amount = request.form.get("min_order_amount", 0, type=float)
32	    delivery_fee = request.form.get("delivery_fee", 0, type=float)
33	
34	    zip_codes = [z.strip() for z in zip_codes_raw.split(",") if z.strip()]
35	
36	    existing = CoverageZone.query.filter_by(name=name).first()
37	    if existing:
38	        flash("A zone with this name already exists.", "danger")
39	        return redirect(url_for("coverage.zones"))
40	
41	    zone = CoverageZone(
42	        name=name,
43	        description=description,
44	        zip_codes_json=zip_codes,
45	        distance_band_min=distance_band_min,
46	        distance_band_max=distance_band_max,
47	        min_order_amount=min_order_amount,
48	        delivery_fee=delivery_fee,
49	    )
50	    db.session.add(zone)
51	    db.session.commit()
52	
53	    log_action("create_zone", "coverage_zone", zone.id, {"name": name})
54	    flash(f"Zone '{name}' created.", "success")
55	    return redirect(url_for("coverage.zones"))
56	
57	
58	@coverage_bp.route("/zones/<int:zone_id>")
59	@role_required("administrator")
60	def zone_detail(zone_id):
61	    zone = db.session.get(CoverageZone, zone_id)
62	    if not zone:
63	        flash("Zone not found.", "danger")
64	        return redirect(url_for("coverage.zones"))
65	    clinicians = Clinician.query.all()
66	    return render_template("coverage/zone_detail.html", zone=zone, clinicians=clinicians)
67	
68	
69	@coverage_bp.route("/zones/<int:zone_id>/assign", methods=["POST"])
70	@role_required("administrator")
71	def assign_clinician(zone_id):
72	    zone = db.session.get(CoverageZone, zone_id)
73	    if not zone:
74	        flash("Zone not found.", "danger")
75	        return redirect(url_for("coverage.zones"))
76	
77	    clinician_id = request.form.get("clinician_id", type=int)
78	    assignment_type = request.form.get("assignment_type", "primary").strip()
79	
80	    if assignment_type not in ("primary", "backup"):
81	        flash("Invalid assignment type.", "danger")
82	        return redirect(url_for("coverage.zone_detail", zone_id=zone_id))
83	
84	    clinician = db.session.get(Clinician, clinician_id)
85	    if not clinician:
86	        flash("Clinician not found.", "danger")
87	        return redirect(url_for("coverage.zone_detail", zone_id=zone_id))
88	
89	    existing = ZoneAssignment.query.filter_by(zone_id=zone_id, clinician_id=clinician_id).first()
90	    if existing:
91	        existing.assignment_type = assignment_type
92	    else:
93	        assignment = ZoneAssignment(zone_id=zone_id, clinician_id=clinician_id, assignment_type=assignment_type)
94	        db.session.add(assignment)
95	
96	    db.session.commit()
97	    log_action("assign_clinician", "coverage_zone", zone_id, {
98	        "clinician_id": clinician_id, "type": assignment_type
99	    })
100	    flash("Clinician assigned to zone.", "success")
101	    return redirect(url_for("coverage.zone_detail", zone_id=zone_id))
102	
103	
104	@coverage_bp.route("/check")
105	def check_coverage():
106	    zip_code = request.args.get("zip", "").strip()
107	    if not zip_code:
108	        return jsonify({"covered": False, "message": "No ZIP code provided"}), 400
109	
110	    zones = CoverageZone.query.filter_by(is_active=True).all()
111	    matching = []
112	    for zone in zones:
113	        zips = zone.zip_codes_json or []
114	        if zip_code in zips:
115	            windows = []
116	            for w in zone.delivery_windows.all():
117	                windows.append({
118	                    "day_of_week": w.day_of_week,
119	                    "start_time": w.start_time.strftime("%H:%M") if w.start_time else None,
120	                    "end_time": w.end_time.strftime("%H:%M") if w.end_time else None,
121	                })
122	            matching.append({
123	                "id": zone.id,
124	                "name": zone.name,
125	                "delivery_fee": zone.delivery_fee,
126	                "min_order_amount": zone.min_order_amount,
127	                "delivery_windows": windows,
128	            })
129	
130	    if matching:
131	        return jsonify({"covered": True, "zones": matching})
132	    return jsonify({"covered": False, "zones": []})
133

1	"""Tests for prompt 05 — health assessments & risk stratification."""
2	
3	import json
4	import pytest
5	from app.models.user import User
6	from app.models.assessment import AssessmentResult, AssessmentDraft
7	from app.extensions import db
8	from app.utils.scoring import calculate_scores, calculate_risk_level
9	
10	
11	def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
12	    with app.app_context():
13	        user = User(username=username, role=role)
14	        user.set_password(password)
15	        db.session.add(user)
16	        db.session.commit()
17	        return user.id
18	
19	
20	def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
21	    return client.post(
22	        "/auth/login",
23	        data={"username": username, "password": password},
24	        follow_redirects=True,
25	    )
26	
27	
28	LOW_RISK_ANSWERS = {
29	    **{f"phq9_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 10)},
30	    **{f"gad7_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 8)},
31	    "bp_category": "Normal",
32	    "fall_history": "no", "mobility_aids": "no", "dizziness": "no", "balance_meds": "no",
33	    "med_adherence": "never_miss",
34	}
35	
36	HIGH_RISK_ANSWERS = {
37	    **{f"phq9_q{i}": "3" for i in range(1, 10)},
38	    **{f"gad7_q{i}": "3" for i in range(1, 8)},
39	    "bp_category": "Crisis",
40	    "fall_history": "yes", "mobility_aids": "yes", "dizziness": "yes", "balance_meds": "yes",
41	    "med_adherence": "often_miss",
42	}
43	
44	
45	# ── Scoring engine unit tests ──
46	
47	def test_scoring_low_risk():
48	    scores = calculate_scores(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
49	    risk, explanations = calculate_risk_level(scores)
50	    assert risk == "Low"
51	    assert scores["phq9"]["total"] == 0
52	    assert scores["gad7"]["total"] == 0
53	
54	
55	def test_scoring_high_risk():
56	    scores = calculate_scores(HIGH_RISK_ANSWERS)
57	    risk, explanations = calculate_risk_level(scores)
58	    assert risk == "High"
59	    assert scores["phq9"]["total"] == 27
60	    assert scores["gad7"]["total"] == 21
61	    assert len(explanations) >= 3
62	
63	
64	def test_scoring_moderate_phq9():
65	    answers = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
66	    # PHQ-9 total = 12 (moderate)
67	    for i in range(1, 5):
68	        answers[f"phq9_q{i}"] = "3"
69	    scores = calculate_scores(answers)
70	    risk, _ = calculate_risk_level(scores)
71	    assert risk == "Moderate"
72	
73	
74	def test_scoring_moderate_bp():
75	    answers = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
76	    answers["bp_category"] = "Stage 1"
77	    scores = calculate_scores(answers)
78	    risk, _ = calculate_risk_level(scores)
79	    assert risk == "Moderate"
80	
81	
82	def test_scoring_high_fall_risk():
83	    answers = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
84	    answers["fall_history"] = "yes"
85	    answers["dizziness"] = "yes"
86	    scores = calculate_scores(answers)
87	    risk, _ = calculate_risk_level(scores)
88	    assert risk == "High"
89	
90	
91	def test_phq9_severity_labels():
92	    for total, expected in [(0, "Minimal"), (5, "Mild"), (10, "Moderate"), (15, "Moderately Severe"), (20, "Severe")]:
93	        answers = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
94	        remaining = total
95	        for i in range(1, 10):
96	            val = min(3, remaining)
97	            answers[f"phq9_q{i}"] = str(val)
98	            remaining -= val
99	        scores = calculate_scores(answers)
100	        assert scores["phq9"]["severity"] == expected, f"Expected {expected} for total {total}"
101	
102	
103	# ── Route tests ──
104	
105	def test_assessment_start_page(client, app):
106	    _create_user(app, "pat_a1")
107	    _login(client, "pat_a1")
108	    resp = client.get("/assessments/start")
109	    assert resp.status_code == 200
110	    assert b"Health Assessment" in resp.data
111	
112	
113	def test_assessment_wizard_step(client, app):
114	    _create_user(app, "pat_a2")
115	    _login(client, "pat_a2")
116	    client.get("/assessments/start")
117	
118	    # Submit step 1 (PHQ-9)
119	    data = {f"phq9_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 10)}
120	    data["request_token"] = "test-token-1"

158	
159	def test_check_coverage_returns_fee_and_minimum(client, app, db):
160	    from datetime import time as dt_time
161	    with app.app_context():
162	        zone = CoverageZone(
163	            name="FeeZone",
164	            zip_codes_json=["70001"],
165	            is_active=True,
166	            delivery_fee=4.99,

All 171 pass. Here's a summary of everything done:

---

## Changes made

### Residual 1 — Template guard on transition buttons
**`app/templates/visits/_visit_rows.html`** — Wrapped transition forms in `{% if current_user.role in ('administrator', 'clinician', 'front_desk') %}`. Patients can never reach the dashboard, but this is now a belt-and-suspenders defence if the partial is ever reused elsewhere.

### Residual 2 — Anti-replay on remaining state-mutating routes
Added `@antireplay` to:
- `POST /assessments/submit` (`routes/assessments.py`)
- `POST /patient/demographics` (`routes/patient.py`)
- `POST /auth/change-password` (`routes/auth.py`)
- `POST /coverage/zones` (`routes/coverage.py`)
- `POST /coverage/zones/<id>/assign` (`routes/coverage.py`)

Also updated `app/utils/antireplay.py` with a **method guard** (`if request.method not in POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE: pass through`) so applying `@antireplay` to GET+POST routes (like `/demographics`) is safe without touching GET handlers.

### Residual 3 — Nonce injection in templates
**`app/__init__.py`** — Added `generate_nonce()` and `generate_timestamp()` Jinja context processor helpers.

**`app/templates/base.html`** — Added a global `htmx:configRequest` listener that injects a fresh `crypto.randomUUID()` nonce and `new Date().toISOString()` timestamp into every HTMX POST automatically. One central fix covers all current and future HTMX forms.

Added server-side `_nonce`/`_timestamp` hidden fields (plain-form / no-JS fallback) to:
- `visits/_visit_rows.html` (transition forms)
- `schedule/available.html` (hold form)
- `schedule/confirm.html` (confirm + cancel forms)
- `schedule/my_appointments.html` (cancel forms)
- `assessments/_review.html` (submit form)
- `patient/_demographics_form.html` (demographics form)
- `auth/change_password.html`
- `coverage/zones.html` (create zone form)
- `coverage/zone_detail.html` (assign clinician form)

### Residual 4 — SignedRequest table growth
**`app/utils/antireplay.py`** — On each protected request, expired rows (`expires_at < now`) are deleted before the nonce check. The table is bounded to ~5 minutes of active nonces with no background job needed.

### Tests updated
`test_demographics.py`, `test_assessments.py`, `test_security.py`, `test_coverage.py` — all POSTs to newly-protected endpoints updated to include `_nonce_data()`.

You are a senior Flask security engineer. Apply a targeted fix for the remaining acceptance gap: signed request validation for anti-replay protection. Keep changes minimal and security-focused. Do not change unrelated business logic or route URLs.
Preserve existing nonce plus timestamp behavior, and add signature validation on top.
Update tests to cover new behavior. Required implementation:

Add signed request validation in anti-replay middleware:
Require nonce, timestamp, and signature for protected state-changing methods.
Keep current replay window and nonce uniqueness checks.
Compute expected signature with HMAC SHA-256 using a server secret.
Include at least method, path, nonce, timestamp in the signing payload.
Reject missing or invalid signature with 400.
Keep replayed nonce as 409.
Keep expired timestamp as 400.
Add server secret configuration:
Introduce a dedicated request signing secret config value.
In production, require this secret to be explicitly set.
Keep development and testing usable with safe defaults for local runs.
Send signature from browser and HTMX requests:
For HTMX POST requests, attach nonce, timestamp, and signature.
For standard forms posting to protected endpoints, ensure hidden fields include nonce, timestamp, and signature.
If client-side signing is used, implement it consistently and document assumptions.
If server-rendered signing is used per form, ensure freshness and compatibility with existing flows.
Preserve compatibility:
Existing CSRF protections must continue to work.
Existing anti-replay nonce storage and cleanup behavior should remain.
Existing authorization and redirect fixes must remain intact.
Required tests:

Add positive and negative tests for signature enforcement:
Missing signature returns 400 on protected endpoint.
Invalid signature returns 400.
Valid signature passes.
Replayed nonce still returns 409.
Cover at least one protected visit endpoint and one other protected endpoint.
Keep existing tests green by updating request helpers where required.
Verification command:

python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e/ -v
Output requirements:

List changed files.
Summarize exact security behavior before and after.
Include test results from the non-Docker command only.
Mention any residual risk clearly as Cannot Confirm if not proven.

13	def antireplay(f):
14	    @wraps(f)
15	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
16	        # Only enforce anti-replay on state-mutating methods.
17	        if request.method not in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE"):
18	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
19	
20	        # Lazily purge expired nonces so the table doesn't grow unbounded.
21	        try:
22	            SignedRequest.query.filter(
23	                SignedRequest.expires_at < datetime.now(timezone.utc)
24	            ).delete()
25	            db.session.commit()
26	        except Exception:
27	            db.session.rollback()
28	
29	        nonce = request.headers.get("X-Nonce") or request.form.get("_nonce")
30	        ts_header = request.headers.get("X-Timestamp") or request.form.get("_timestamp")
31	        if not nonce or not ts_header:
32	            return jsonify({"error": "Missing nonce or timestamp"}), 400
33	        try:
34	            ts = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_header)
35	            if ts.tzinfo is None:
36	                ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
37	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
38	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
39	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
40	        if abs(now - ts) > REPLAY_WINDOW:
41	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
42	        existing = SignedRequest.query.filter_by(nonce=nonce).first()
43	        if existing:
44	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 409
45	        sr = SignedRequest(nonce=nonce, timestamp=ts, expires_at=ts + REPLAY_WINDOW)
46	        db.session.add(sr)
47	        db.session.commit()
48	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
49	    return decorated
50

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\tests\signing_helpers.py", "content": """"Shared test helpers for anti-replay signed request data.

The secret must match TestingConfig.REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET exactly so that the
decorator's HMAC verification passes in test runs.
"""
import hmac
import hashlib
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone

TEST_SIGNING_SECRET = "test-request-signing-secret-dev-only"


def signed_data(method: str, path: str, extra: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    """Return a form-data dict with _nonce, _timestamp, and _signature.

    Parameters
    ----------
    method : HTTP verb (e.g. "POST")
    path   : URL path exactly as Flask will see it in request.path
             (e.g. "/visits/1/transition")
    extra  : additional form fields to merge in (e.g. {"target_state": "checked_in"})
    """
    nonce = str(uuid.uuid4())
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
    sig = hmac.new(
        TEST_SIGNING_SECRET.encode(),
        payload.encode(),
        hashlib.sha256,
    ).hexdigest()
    data = {"_nonce": nonce, "_timestamp": timestamp, "_signature": sig}
    if extra:
        data.update(extra)
    return data
"}

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The conversation covers three sequential rounds of security fixes to a MeridianCare Flask+HTMX clinic operations platform:

**Round 1** (from `fix_implementation_prompt`): Fix acceptance audit findings — enforce visit authorization, enforce anti-replay (no silent bypass), prevent open redirects on login `next`, and require stable production keys. Add regression tests for all.

**Round 2** ("Fix the Residual Risk"): Address four leftover risks from Round 1: (1) template guard on transition buttons for non-staff roles, (2) add `@antireplay` to remaining state-mutating routes (assessments/submit, patient/demographics, auth/change-password, coverage CRUD), (3) inject nonce+timestamp into all protected form templates, (4) add lazy cleanup of expired `SignedRequest` rows.

**Round 3** (most recent, in-progress): Add HMAC-SHA256 **signature** validation on top of existing nonce+timestamp anti-replay. Signing payload = `METHOD|path|nonce|timestamp`. New `REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET` config key (stable in testing, required in production). All three hidden fields `_nonce`, `_timestamp`, `_signature` must be server-rendered per form (the JS `htmx:configRequest` nonce-regeneration handler is removed because it would invalidate the server-computed signature). Missing/invalid signature → 400. Replayed nonce → 409. Update all tests to use a shared `signed_data()` helper.

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- Flask application factory pattern (`create_app(env)`)
- HTMX server-side rendering and form submission
- Anti-replay protection: nonce uniqueness + timestamp window (5 min) + HMAC-SHA256 signature
- HMAC-SHA256: `hmac.new(secret.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()`
- `hmac.compare_digest()` for constant-time signature comparison
- Flask `@role_required` decorator, `@login_required`, `@antireplay` decorator stacking
- Jinja2 `Markup` for server-rendered signed hidden fields
- Flask context processors returning callables to templates
- `ProductionConfig.validate()` static method called in `create_app()`
- `SignedRequest` SQLAlchemy model (nonce, timestamp, expires_at) for replay tracking
- Lazy nonce cleanup: delete expired rows on each protected request
- `_is_safe_redirect_url()` for open-redirect prevention (no scheme, no netloc, starts with `/`)
- Fernet encryption for patient sensitive fields
- Flask-WTF CSRF protection (separate from anti-replay)
- pytest with SQLite in-memory database per test

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`app/utils/antireplay.py`** (most recently modified — Round 3 complete)
  - Added `_compute_signature()` helper
  - Added `X-Signature` / `_signature` extraction
  - Added 400 for missing signature
  - Added HMAC verification with `hmac.compare_digest()` BEFORE replay check
  - Method guard (skip GET/HEAD), lazy cleanup still present
  ```python
  def _compute_signature(secret: str, method: str, path: str, nonce: str, timestamp: str) -> str:
      payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
      return hmac.new(secret.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()

  def antireplay(f):
      @wraps(f)
      def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
          if request.method not in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE"):
              return f(*args, **kwargs)
          # lazy cleanup...
          nonce = request.headers.get("X-Nonce") or request.form.get("_nonce")
          ts_header = request.headers.get("X-Timestamp") or request.form.get("_timestamp")
          signature = request.headers.get("X-Signature") or request.form.get("_signature")
          if not nonce or not ts_header:
              return jsonify({"error": "Missing nonce or timestamp"}), 400
          if not signature:
              return jsonify({"error": "Missing request signature"}), 400
          # timestamp parse + window check...
          secret = current_app.config.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "")
          expected = _compute_signature(secret, request.method, request.path, nonce, ts_header)
          if not hmac.compare_digest(signature, expected):
              return jsonify({"error": "Invalid request signature"}), 400
          existing = SignedRequest.query.filter_by(nonce=nonce).first()
          if existing:
              return jsonify({"error": "Request already processed"}), 409
          # store nonce...
  ```

- **`app/config.py`** (Round 3 complete)
  - `Config.REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET = os.environ.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", os.urandom(32).hex())`
  - `TestingConfig.REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET = "test-request-signing-secret-dev-only"` (stable, known)
  - `ProductionConfig.validate()` now checks `SECRET_KEY`, `ENCRYPTION_KEY`, `REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET`

- **`app/__init__.py`** (Round 3 complete)
  - `antireplay_inputs(method, path)` context processor returning `Markup` with all three hidden fields
  - Uses `app.config.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "")` (closure captures app instance)
  - Kept `generate_nonce()` and `generate_timestamp()` as backward-compat helpers
  ```python
  def antireplay_inputs(method, path):
      nonce = str(uuid.uuid4())
      timestamp = datetime.now(_tz.utc).isoformat()
      secret = app.config.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "")
      payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
      sig = _hmac.new(secret.encode(), payload.encode(), _hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
      return Markup(
          f'<input type="hidden" name="_nonce" value="{nonce}">'
          f'<input type="hidden" name="_timestamp" value="{timestamp}">'
          f'<input type="hidden" name="_signature" value="{sig}">'
      )
  ```

  ```python
  def signed_data(method: str, path: str, extra: dict | None = None) -> dict:
      nonce = str(uuid.uuid4())
      timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
      payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
      sig = hmac.new(TEST_SIGNING_SECRET.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
      data = {"_nonce": nonce, "_timestamp": timestamp, "_signature": sig}
      if extra:
          data.update(extra)
      return data
  ```

  - Still missing `os.environ.setdefault("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "...")` for production app creation test

- **`app/routes/visits.py`** (Round 1+2 changes)
  - `transition`: `@role_required("administrator", "clinician", "front_desk")` + `@antireplay`
  - `timeline`: patient ownership check → `abort(403)` if not staff and not own visit
  - Imports `antireplay` from `app.utils.antireplay`

- **`app/routes/auth.py`** (Round 1+3 changes)
  - Added `_is_safe_redirect_url()`: checks no scheme, no netloc, starts with `/`
  - `login()`: `destination = next_page if _is_safe_redirect_url(next_page) else url_for(...)`
  - Added `@antireplay` to `change_password()`
  - Imports `antireplay`

- **`app/routes/schedule.py`** (Round 1+2 changes)
  - `@antireplay` on `hold()`, `confirm()`, `cancel()`

- **`app/routes/patient.py`** (Round 1+2 changes)
  - `@antireplay` on `delete_account()` and `demographics()`

- **`app/routes/assessments.py`** (Round 2 changes)
  - `@antireplay` on `submit()`

- **`app/routes/coverage.py`** (Round 2 changes)
  - `@antireplay` on `create_zone()` and `assign_clinician()`

---

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **Round 1**: `test_app_factory_configs` in `test_foundation.py` failed with `RuntimeError: Production requires these environment variables: SECRET_KEY, ENCRYPTION_KEY` because the test tried to `create_app("production")` without env vars. Fixed by adding:
  ```python
  os.environ.setdefault("SECRET_KEY", "stable-test-secret-key-1234567890ab")
  os.environ.setdefault("ENCRYPTION_KEY", Fernet.generate_key().decode())
  ```

- **Round 2**: `test_change_log_created` in `test_demographics.py` was missed in the initial update pass — it had two `client.post("/patient/demographics", ...)` calls without nonce. Fixed by adding `_nonce_data()` to both.

- **Round 3**: Removed the `htmx:configRequest` JS handler from `base.html` because it regenerated `_nonce` and `_timestamp` on every HTMX POST, which would invalidate the server-computed HMAC signature that was already in the form's hidden fields.

---

5. Problem Solving:

- **Anti-replay bypass**: Removed `return f(*args, **kwargs)` graceful fallback when nonce/timestamp missing; now returns 400.
- **Method guard**: Added `if request.method not in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE"): return f(*args, **kwargs)` so `@antireplay` on GET+POST routes (like `/patient/demographics`) doesn't block GET requests.
- **Nonce table growth**: Lazy `SignedRequest.query.filter(SignedRequest.expires_at < now).delete()` at start of each protected request.
- **Signature order**: Signature check occurs BEFORE nonce replay check to prevent attacker from probing nonce existence via 409 vs 400 status codes.
- **Server-side signing**: Since `REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET` must never reach the browser, all signing is done in Jinja2 context processor `antireplay_inputs(method, path)` returning `Markup`.
- **Template URL consistency**: Each template captures `url_for(...)` into a Jinja variable (e.g., `{% set _action = ... %}`) and passes the same value to both the form `action` attribute and `antireplay_inputs()`.

---

- **Run verification**: `python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e/ -v`

1	"""Regression tests for acceptance audit findings.
2	
3	Covers:
4	  - Visit authorization (patient cannot transition or view others' timelines)
5	  - Anti-replay enforcement (missing/replayed nonce rejected)
6	  - Open redirect prevention on login next parameter
7	  - Production config requires stable keys
8	"""
9	
10	import os
11	import uuid
12	import pytest
13	from datetime import datetime, timezone
14	
15	from app.models.user import User
16	from app.models.scheduling import Clinician
17	from app.models.visit import Visit
18	from app.extensions import db as _db
19	
20	
21	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
22	# Helpers
23	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	
25	def _nonce_data():
26	    return {
27	        "_nonce": str(uuid.uuid4()),
28	        "_timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
29	    }
30	
31	
32	def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
33	    with app.app_context():
34	        user = User(username=username, role=role)
35	        user.set_password(password)
36	        _db.session.add(user)
37	        _db.session.commit()
38	        return user.id
39	
40	
41	def _create_clinician(app, username="doc_audit"):
42	    with app.app_context():
43	        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
44	        user.set_password("Password1")
45	        _db.session.add(user)
46	        _db.session.commit()
47	        clinician = Clinician(user_id=user.id, specialty="General")
48	        _db.session.add(clinician)
49	        _db.session.commit()
50	        return user.id, clinician.id
51	
52	
53	def _create_visit(app, patient_id, clinician_id, status="booked"):
54	    with app.app_context():
55	        visit = Visit(patient_id=patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id, status=status)
56	        _db.session.add(visit)
57	        _db.session.commit()
58	        return visit.id
59	
60	
61	def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
62	    return client.post(
63	        "/auth/login",
64	        data={"username": username, "password": password},
65	        follow_redirects=True,
66	    )
67	
68	
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	# A) Visit authorization — negative tests
71	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
72	
73	class TestVisitAuthorization:
74	
75	    def test_patient_cannot_transition_arbitrary_visit(self, client, app, db):
76	        """Patient role must be rejected with 403 on visit transition."""
77	        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz1")
78	        other_pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_other1")
79	        attacker_id = _create_user(app, "pat_attacker1")
80	        vid = _create_visit(app, other_pat_id, cid)
81	
82	        _login(client, "pat_attacker1")
83	        resp = client.post(
84	            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
85	            data={"target_state": "checked_in", **_nonce_data()},
86	        )
87	        assert resp.status_code == 403
88	
89	    def test_patient_cannot_view_other_patient_timeline(self, client, app, db):
90	        """Patient may not view timeline for a visit that belongs to someone else."""
91	        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz2")
92	        owner_id = _create_user(app, "pat_owner2")
93	        spy_id = _create_user(app, "pat_spy2")
94	        vid = _create_visit(app, owner_id, cid)
95	
96	        _login(client, "pat_spy2")
97	        resp = client.get(f"/visits/{vid}/timeline")
98	        assert resp.status_code == 403
99	
100	    def test_patient_can_view_own_timeline(self, client, app, db):
101	        """Patient may view timeline for their own visit."""
102	        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz3")
103	        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_own3")
104	        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
105	
106	        _login(client, "pat_own3")
107	        resp = client.get(f"/visits/{vid}/timeline")
108	        assert resp.status_code == 200
109	
110	    def test_staff_can_transition_visit(self, client, app, db):
111	        """Staff role should succeed on transition when valid nonce provided."""
112	        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz4")
113	        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_authz4")
114	        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_authz4", role="administrator")
115	        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
116	
117	        _login(client, "admin_authz4")
118	        resp = client.post(
119	            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
120	            data={"target_state": "checked_in", **_nonce_data()},
121	            follow_redirects=True,
122	        )
123	        assert resp.status_code == 200

126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	# B) Anti-replay tests
128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	class TestAntiReplay:
131	
132	    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_visit_transition(self, client, app, db):
133	        """Transition without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
134	        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar1")
135	        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar1")
136	        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar1", role="administrator")
137	        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
138	
139	        _login(client, "admin_ar1")
140	        resp = client.post(
141	            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
142	            data={"target_state": "checked_in"},
143	        )
144	        assert resp.status_code == 400
145	
146	    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_schedule_hold(self, client, app, db):
147	        """Hold without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
148	        from app.models.scheduling import Slot
149	        from datetime import date, time, timedelta
150	
151	        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar2")
152	        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar2")
153	
154	        with app.app_context():
155	            slot = Slot(
156	                clinician_id=cid,
157	                date=date.today() + timedelta(days=1),
158	                start_time=time(9, 0),
159	                end_time=time(9, 15),
160	                capacity=1,
161	            )
162	            _db.session.add(slot)
163	            _db.session.commit()
164	            sid = slot.id
165	
166	        _login(client, "pat_ar2")
167	        resp = client.post(f"/schedule/hold/{sid}")
168	        assert resp.status_code == 400
169	
170	    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_delete_account(self, client, app, db):
171	        """Account deletion without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
172	        _create_user(app, "pat_ar3")
173	        _login(client, "pat_ar3")
174	        resp = client.post("/patient/delete-account", data={"password": "Password1"})
175	        assert resp.status_code == 400
176	
177	    def test_replayed_nonce_rejected(self, client, app, db):
178	        """Reusing the same nonce on two requests must return 409 on the second."""
179	        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar4")
180	        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar4")
181	        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar4", role="administrator")
182	        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
183	
184	        _login(client, "admin_ar4")
185	        nonce_payload = _nonce_data()
186	
187	        # First request — should succeed
188	        resp1 = client.post(
189	            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
190	            data={"target_state": "checked_in", **nonce_payload},
191	            follow_redirects=False,
192	        )
193	        assert resp1.status_code != 409
194	
195	        # Replay the same nonce — second visit in state checked_in -> seen
196	        # (we need a visit still in a transitionable state for the replay to be
197	        # meaningful, but the important thing is the 409 nonce check fires first)
198	        vid2 = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
199	        resp2 = client.post(
200	            f"/visits/{vid2}/transition",
201	            data={"target_state": "checked_in", **nonce_payload},
202	        )
203	        assert resp2.status_code == 409

257

1	import hmac
2	import hashlib
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
4	from flask import request, current_app, jsonify
5	from functools import wraps
6	from app.extensions import db
7	from app.models.audit import SignedRequest
8	
9	REPLAY_WINDOW = timedelta(minutes=5)
10	
11	
12	def _compute_signature(secret: str, method: str, path: str, nonce: str, timestamp: str) -> str:
13	    """HMAC-SHA256 over 'METHOD|path|nonce|timestamp' using the server signing secret."""
14	    payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
15	    return hmac.new(secret.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
16	
17	
18	def antireplay(f):
19	    @wraps(f)
20	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
21	        # Only enforce anti-replay on state-mutating methods.
22	        if request.method not in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE"):
23	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
24	
25	        # Lazily purge expired nonces so the table doesn't grow unbounded.
26	        try:
27	            SignedRequest.query.filter(
28	                SignedRequest.expires_at < datetime.now(timezone.utc)
29	            ).delete()
30	            db.session.commit()
31	        except Exception:
32	            db.session.rollback()
33	
34	        nonce = request.headers.get("X-Nonce") or request.form.get("_nonce")
35	        ts_header = request.headers.get("X-Timestamp") or request.form.get("_timestamp")
36	        signature = request.headers.get("X-Signature") or request.form.get("_signature")
37	
38	        if not nonce or not ts_header:
39	            return jsonify({"error": "Missing nonce or timestamp"}), 400
40	
41	        if not signature:
42	            return jsonify({"error": "Missing request signature"}), 400
43	
44	        try:
45	            ts = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_header)
46	            if ts.tzinfo is None:
47	                ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
48	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
49	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
50	
51	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
52	        if abs(now - ts) > REPLAY_WINDOW:
53	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
54	
55	        # Signature verification — must happen before the nonce is stored so
56	        # an attacker cannot use a 409 response to probe whether a nonce exists.
57	        secret = current_app.config.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "")
58	        expected = _compute_signature(secret, request.method, request.path, nonce, ts_header)
59	        if not hmac.compare_digest(signature, expected):
60	            return jsonify({"error": "Invalid request signature"}), 400
61	
62	        # Replay check — nonce must not have been seen before.
63	        existing = SignedRequest.query.filter_by(nonce=nonce).first()
64	        if existing:
65	            return jsonify({"error": "Request already processed"}), 409
66	
67	        sr = SignedRequest(nonce=nonce, timestamp=ts, expires_at=ts + REPLAY_WINDOW)
68	        db.session.add(sr)
69	        db.session.commit()
70	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
71	    return decorated
72

{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\tests\test_acceptance_audit.py", "content": """"Regression tests for acceptance audit findings.

Covers:
  - Visit authorization (patient cannot transition or view others' timelines)
  - Anti-replay enforcement (missing/replayed nonce rejected, signature validation)
  - Open redirect prevention on login next parameter
  - Production config requires stable keys
"""

import os
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone

from app.models.user import User
from app.models.scheduling import Clinician
from app.models.visit import Visit
from app.extensions import db as _db
from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role=role)
        user.set_password(password)
        _db.session.add(user)
        _db.session.commit()
        return user.id


def _create_clinician(app, username="doc_audit"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
        user.set_password("Password1")
        _db.session.add(user)
        _db.session.commit()
        clinician = Clinician(user_id=user.id, specialty="General")
        _db.session.add(clinician)
        _db.session.commit()
        return user.id, clinician.id


def _create_visit(app, patient_id, clinician_id, status="booked"):
    with app.app_context():
        visit = Visit(patient_id=patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id, status=status)
        _db.session.add(visit)
        _db.session.commit()
        return visit.id


def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# A) Visit authorization — negative tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestVisitAuthorization:

    def test_patient_cannot_transition_arbitrary_visit(self, client, app, db):
        """Patient role must be rejected with 403 on visit transition."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz1")
        other_pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_other1")
        attacker_id = _create_user(app, "pat_attacker1")
        vid = _create_visit(app, other_pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "pat_attacker1")
        resp = client.post(
            path,
            data=signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"}),
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_patient_cannot_view_other_patient_timeline(self, client, app, db):
        """Patient may not view timeline for a visit that belongs to someone else."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz2")
        owner_id = _create_user(app, "pat_owner2")
        spy_id = _create_user(app, "pat_spy2")
        vid = _create_visit(app, owner_id, cid)

        _login(client, "pat_spy2")
        resp = client.get(f"/visits/{vid}/timeline")
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_patient_can_view_own_timeline(self, client, app, db):
        """Patient may view timeline for their own visit."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz3")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_own3")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)

        _login(client, "pat_own3")
        resp = client.get(f"/visits/{vid}/timeline")
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_staff_can_transition_visit(self, client, app, db):
        """Staff role should succeed on transition when valid signed request provided."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz4")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_authz4")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_authz4", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_authz4")
        resp = client.post(
            path,
            data=signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"}),
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# B) Anti-replay tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAntiReplay:

    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_visit_transition(self, client, app, db):
        """Transition without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar1")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar1")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar1", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)

        _login(client, "admin_ar1")
        resp = client.post(
            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
            data={"target_state": "checked_in"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_schedule_hold(self, client, app, db):
        """Hold without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
        from app.models.scheduling import Slot
        from datetime import date, time, timedelta

        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar2")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar2")

        with app.app_context():
            slot = Slot(
                clinician_id=cid,
                date=date.today() + timedelta(days=1),
                start_time=time(9, 0),
                end_time=time(9, 15),
                capacity=1,
            )
            _db.session.add(slot)
            _db.session.commit()
            sid = slot.id

        _login(client, "pat_ar2")
        resp = client.post(f"/schedule/hold/{sid}")
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_delete_account(self, client, app, db):
        """Account deletion without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_ar3")
        _login(client, "pat_ar3")
        resp = client.post("/patient/delete-account", data={"password": "Password1"})
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_replayed_nonce_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """Reusing the same nonce on the same path must return 409 on the second request."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar4")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar4")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar4", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_ar4")
        payload = signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"})

        # First request — should succeed (nonce is fresh)
        resp1 = client.post(path, data=payload, follow_redirects=False)
        assert resp1.status_code != 409

        # Replay the exact same signed data — nonce already consumed → 409
        resp2 = client.post(path, data=payload)
        assert resp2.status_code == 409

    def test_missing_signature_returns_400(self, client, app, db):
        """POST with nonce+timestamp but no signature must return 400."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar5")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar5")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar5", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)

        _login(client, "admin_ar5")
        resp = client.post(
            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
            data={
                "target_state": "checked_in",
                "_nonce": str(uuid.uuid4()),
                "_timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            },
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_invalid_signature_returns_400(self, client, app, db):
        """POST with a tampered signature must return 400."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar6")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar6")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar6", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_ar6")
        data = signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"})
        data["_signature"] = "deadbeef" * 8  # tampered — wrong HMAC
        resp = client.post(path, data=data)
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_valid_signature_passes(self, client, app, db):
        """POST with a correct signature and fresh nonce must be accepted."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar7")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar7")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar7", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_ar7")
        resp = client.post(
            path,
            data=signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"}),
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# C) Open redirect tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestOpenRedirect:

    def _register_and_login_user(self, client, app, username):
        """Register a fresh user and return a logged-in client."""
        _create_user(app, username)

    def test_external_next_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """Login with external next URL must NOT redirect to that URL."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir1")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=http://evil.example.com/steal",
            data={"username": "pat_redir1", "password": "Password1"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (302, 200)
        location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "evil.example.com" not in location

    def test_external_next_with_double_slash_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """Protocol-relative URL (//evil.example.com) must also be blocked."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir2")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=//evil.example.com/steal",
            data={"username": "pat_redir2", "password": "Password2Aa"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        # If login fails due to password, re-do with correct password
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir2b")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=//evil.example.com/steal",
            data={"username": "pat_redir2b", "password": "Password1"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "evil.example.com" not in location

    def test_relative_internal_next_allowed(self, client, app, db):
        """A safe relative path in next must be honoured after login."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir3")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=/patient/demographics",
            data={"username": "pat_redir3", "password": "Password1"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 302
        location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "/patient/demographics" in location

    def test_htmx_login_external_next_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """HTMX login path: external next must not appear in HX-Redirect header."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir4")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=https://evil.example.com/",
            data={"username": "pat_redir4", "password": "Password1"},
            headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
        )
        hx_redirect = resp.headers.get("HX-Redirect", "")
        assert "evil.example.com" not in hx_redirect


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# D) Production config tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestProductionConfig:

    def test_production_fails_without_secret_key(self):
        """create_app('production') raises RuntimeError when SECRET_KEY is absent."""
        from app import create_app

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        try:
            with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="SECRET_KEY"):
                create_app("production")
        finally:
            for key, val in env_backup.items():
                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val

    def test_production_fails_without_encryption_key(self):
        """create_app('production') raises RuntimeError when ENCRYPTION_KEY is absent."""
        from app import create_app
        from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        os.environ["SECRET_KEY"] = "stable-test-secret-key-x1234567890"
        os.environ["REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"] = "stable-test-signing-secret-x1234"
        try:
            with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="ENCRYPTION_KEY"):
                create_app("production")
        finally:
            os.environ.pop("SECRET_KEY", None)
            os.environ.pop("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", None)
            for key, val in env_backup.items():
                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val

    def test_production_fails_without_signing_secret(self):
        """create_app('production') raises RuntimeError when REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET is absent."""
        from app import create_app
        from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        os.environ["SECRET_KEY"] = "stable-test-secret-key-x1234567890"
        os.environ["ENCRYPTION_KEY"] = Fernet.generate_key().decode()
        try:
            with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
                create_app("production")
        finally:
            os.environ.pop("SECRET_KEY", None)
            os.environ.pop("ENCRYPTION_KEY", None)
            for key, val in env_backup.items():
                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val

    def test_production_succeeds_with_all_keys(self):
        """create_app('production') succeeds when all required keys are supplied."""
        from app import create_app
        from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        os.environ["SECRET_KEY"] = "stable-test-secret-key-x1234567890"
        os.environ["ENCRYPTION_KEY"] = Fernet.generate_key().decode()
        os.environ["REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"] = "stable-test-signing-secret-x1234"
        try:
            app = create_app("production")
            assert app is not None
        finally:
            os.environ.pop("SECRET_KEY", None)

            os.environ.pop("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", None)

                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val

function.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\tests\test_acceptance_audit.py", "content": """"Regression tests for acceptance audit findings.

Covers:
  - Visit authorization (patient cannot transition or view others' timelines)
  - Anti-replay enforcement (missing/replayed nonce rejected, signature validation)
  - Open redirect prevention on login next parameter
  - Production config requires stable keys
"""

import os
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone

from app.models.user import User
from app.models.scheduling import Clinician
from app.models.visit import Visit
from app.extensions import db as _db
from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role=role)
        user.set_password(password)
        _db.session.add(user)
        _db.session.commit()
        return user.id


def _create_clinician(app, username="doc_audit"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
        user.set_password("Password1")
        _db.session.add(user)
        _db.session.commit()
        clinician = Clinician(user_id=user.id, specialty="General")
        _db.session.add(clinician)
        _db.session.commit()
        return user.id, clinician.id


def _create_visit(app, patient_id, clinician_id, status="booked"):
    with app.app_context():
        visit = Visit(patient_id=patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id, status=status)
        _db.session.add(visit)
        _db.session.commit()
        return visit.id


def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# A) Visit authorization — negative tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestVisitAuthorization:

    def test_patient_cannot_transition_arbitrary_visit(self, client, app, db):
        """Patient role must be rejected with 403 on visit transition."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz1")
        other_pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_other1")
        attacker_id = _create_user(app, "pat_attacker1")
        vid = _create_visit(app, other_pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "pat_attacker1")
        resp = client.post(
            path,
            data=signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"}),
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_patient_cannot_view_other_patient_timeline(self, client, app, db):
        """Patient may not view timeline for a visit that belongs to someone else."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz2")
        owner_id = _create_user(app, "pat_owner2")
        spy_id = _create_user(app, "pat_spy2")
        vid = _create_visit(app, owner_id, cid)

        _login(client, "pat_spy2")
        resp = client.get(f"/visits/{vid}/timeline")
        assert resp.status_code == 403

    def test_patient_can_view_own_timeline(self, client, app, db):
        """Patient may view timeline for their own visit."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz3")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_own3")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)

        _login(client, "pat_own3")
        resp = client.get(f"/visits/{vid}/timeline")
        assert resp.status_code == 200

    def test_staff_can_transition_visit(self, client, app, db):
        """Staff role should succeed on transition when valid signed request provided."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_authz4")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_authz4")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_authz4", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_authz4")
        resp = client.post(
            path,
            data=signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"}),
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# B) Anti-replay tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAntiReplay:

    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_visit_transition(self, client, app, db):
        """Transition without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar1")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar1")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar1", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)

        _login(client, "admin_ar1")
        resp = client.post(
            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
            data={"target_state": "checked_in"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_schedule_hold(self, client, app, db):
        """Hold without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
        from app.models.scheduling import Slot
        from datetime import date, time, timedelta

        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar2")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar2")

        with app.app_context():
            slot = Slot(
                clinician_id=cid,
                date=date.today() + timedelta(days=1),
                start_time=time(9, 0),
                end_time=time(9, 15),
                capacity=1,
            )
            _db.session.add(slot)
            _db.session.commit()
            sid = slot.id

        _login(client, "pat_ar2")
        resp = client.post(f"/schedule/hold/{sid}")
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_missing_nonce_rejected_on_delete_account(self, client, app, db):
        """Account deletion without nonce/timestamp must return 400."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_ar3")
        _login(client, "pat_ar3")
        resp = client.post("/patient/delete-account", data={"password": "Password1"})
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_replayed_nonce_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """Reusing the same nonce on the same path must return 409 on the second request."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar4")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar4")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar4", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_ar4")
        payload = signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"})

        # First request — should succeed (nonce is fresh)
        resp1 = client.post(path, data=payload, follow_redirects=False)
        assert resp1.status_code != 409

        # Replay the exact same signed data — nonce already consumed → 409
        resp2 = client.post(path, data=payload)
        assert resp2.status_code == 409

    def test_missing_signature_returns_400(self, client, app, db):
        """POST with nonce+timestamp but no signature must return 400."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar5")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar5")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar5", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)

        _login(client, "admin_ar5")
        resp = client.post(
            f"/visits/{vid}/transition",
            data={
                "target_state": "checked_in",
                "_nonce": str(uuid.uuid4()),
                "_timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            },
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_invalid_signature_returns_400(self, client, app, db):
        """POST with a tampered signature must return 400."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar6")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar6")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar6", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_ar6")
        data = signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"})
        data["_signature"] = "deadbeef" * 8  # tampered — wrong HMAC
        resp = client.post(path, data=data)
        assert resp.status_code == 400

    def test_valid_signature_passes(self, client, app, db):
        """POST with a correct signature and fresh nonce must be accepted."""
        _, cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ar7")
        pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_ar7")
        admin_id = _create_user(app, "admin_ar7", role="administrator")
        vid = _create_visit(app, pat_id, cid)
        path = f"/visits/{vid}/transition"

        _login(client, "admin_ar7")
        resp = client.post(
            path,
            data=signed_data("POST", path, {"target_state": "checked_in"}),
            follow_redirects=True,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# C) Open redirect tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestOpenRedirect:

    def _register_and_login_user(self, client, app, username):
        """Register a fresh user and return a logged-in client."""
        _create_user(app, username)

    def test_external_next_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """Login with external next URL must NOT redirect to that URL."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir1")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=http://evil.example.com/steal",
            data={"username": "pat_redir1", "password": "Password1"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (302, 200)
        location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "evil.example.com" not in location

    def test_external_next_with_double_slash_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """Protocol-relative URL (//evil.example.com) must also be blocked."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir2")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=//evil.example.com/steal",
            data={"username": "pat_redir2", "password": "Password2Aa"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        # If login fails due to password, re-do with correct password
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir2b")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=//evil.example.com/steal",
            data={"username": "pat_redir2b", "password": "Password1"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "evil.example.com" not in location

    def test_relative_internal_next_allowed(self, client, app, db):
        """A safe relative path in next must be honoured after login."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir3")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=/patient/demographics",
            data={"username": "pat_redir3", "password": "Password1"},
            follow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 302
        location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
        assert "/patient/demographics" in location

    def test_htmx_login_external_next_rejected(self, client, app, db):
        """HTMX login path: external next must not appear in HX-Redirect header."""
        _create_user(app, "pat_redir4")
        resp = client.post(
            "/auth/login?next=https://evil.example.com/",
            data={"username": "pat_redir4", "password": "Password1"},
            headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
        )
        hx_redirect = resp.headers.get("HX-Redirect", "")
        assert "evil.example.com" not in hx_redirect


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# D) Production config tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestProductionConfig:

    def test_production_fails_without_secret_key(self):
        """create_app('production') raises RuntimeError when SECRET_KEY is absent."""
        from app import create_app

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        try:
            with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="SECRET_KEY"):
                create_app("production")
        finally:
            for key, val in env_backup.items():
                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val

    def test_production_fails_without_encryption_key(self):
        """create_app('production') raises RuntimeError when ENCRYPTION_KEY is absent."""
        from app import create_app
        from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        os.environ["SECRET_KEY"] = "stable-test-secret-key-x1234567890"
        os.environ["REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"] = "stable-test-signing-secret-x1234"
        try:
            with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="ENCRYPTION_KEY"):
                create_app("production")
        finally:
            os.environ.pop("SECRET_KEY", None)
            os.environ.pop("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", None)
            for key, val in env_backup.items():
                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val

    def test_production_fails_without_signing_secret(self):
        """create_app('production') raises RuntimeError when REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET is absent."""
        from app import create_app
        from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        os.environ["SECRET_KEY"] = "stable-test-secret-key-x1234567890"
        os.environ["ENCRYPTION_KEY"] = Fernet.generate_key().decode()
        try:
            with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
                create_app("production")
        finally:
            os.environ.pop("SECRET_KEY", None)
            os.environ.pop("ENCRYPTION_KEY", None)
            for key, val in env_backup.items():
                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val

    def test_production_succeeds_with_all_keys(self):
        """create_app('production') succeeds when all required keys are supplied."""
        from app import create_app
        from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

        env_backup = {}
        for key in ("SECRET_KEY", "ENCRYPTION_KEY", "REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"):
            env_backup[key] = os.environ.pop(key, None)

        os.environ["SECRET_KEY"] = "stable-test-secret-key-x1234567890"
        os.environ["ENCRYPTION_KEY"] = Fernet.generate_key().decode()
        os.environ["REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET"] = "stable-test-signing-secret-x1234"
        try:
            app = create_app("production")
            assert app is not None
        finally:
            os.environ.pop("SECRET_KEY", None)
            os.environ.pop("ENCRYPTION_KEY", None)
            os.environ.pop("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", None)
            for key, val in env_backup.items():
                if val is not None:
                    os.environ[key] = val
"}

content.text:
1	# MeridianCare Clinic Operations Platform
2	
3	A comprehensive clinic operations platform built with Flask, SQLite, and HTMX.
4	
5	## Requirements
6	
7	- Python 3.10+
8	- Docker (for E2E tests)
9	
10	## Environment Variables (Production)
11	
12	The following environment variables **must** be set when running with `FLASK_ENV=production`:
13	
14	| Variable | Description |
15	|---|---|
16	| `SECRET_KEY` | Stable secret key for Flask session signing (e.g. `python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"`) |
17	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | Stable Fernet key for field-level encryption (e.g. `python -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())"`) |
18	
19	The app will raise `RuntimeError` at startup if either is missing in production.
20	In development and testing, random/default keys are generated automatically.
21	
22	## Quick Start
23	
24	```bash
25	# Install dependencies
26	pip install -r requirements.txt
27	
28	# Run the application (HTTPS on port 5000)
29	python run.py
30	```
31	
32	Visit `https://localhost:5000` in your browser. Accept the self-signed certificate warning.
33	
34	## Running Tests
35	
36	### Unit/Integration Tests Only
37	
38	```bash
39	pip install -r requirements-test.txt
40	python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e/ -v
41	```
42	
43	### Full Test Suite (Unit + E2E via Docker)
44	
45	```bash
46	bash run_tests.sh
47	```
48	
49	This script will:
50	1. Run unit/integration tests via pytest
51	2. Check that Docker is running
52	3. Build and start the app in a Docker container
53	4. Wait for the `/health` endpoint to respond
54	5. Run Playwright E2E tests against the containerized app
55	6. Tear down the Docker container
56	7. Exit with code 0 on success, non-zero on failure
57	
58	## Project Structure
59	
60	```
61	repo/
62	├── app/
63	│   ├── __init__.py          # App factory
64	│   ├── config.py            # Configuration classes
65	│   ├── extensions.py        # Flask extensions
66	│   ├── models/              # Database models
67	│   ├── routes/              # Blueprint route modules
68	│   ├── templates/           # Jinja2 templates
69	│   ├── static/              # CSS, JS, images
70	│   └── utils/               # Helpers (logging, crypto, etc.)
71	├── migrations/              # DB migration scripts
72	├── certs/                   # Self-signed certificates (auto-generated)
73	├── tests/                   # Unit & integration tests
74	│   └── e2e/                 # Playwright E2E tests
75	├── run_tests.sh             # Single script to run ALL tests
76	├── Dockerfile               # Container for E2E testing
77	├── docker-compose.yml       # Docker Compose config
78	├── requirements.txt         # Production dependencies
79	├── requirements-test.txt    # Test dependencies
80	└── run.py                   # Entry point
81	```
82

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
