def test_dismiss_reminder_succeeds_with_antireplay(client, app, db):
    """POST /reminders/<id>/dismiss with signed fields dismisses the reminder."""
    from app.models.reminder import Reminder
    pat_id = _create_user(app, "pat_dis2", role="patient")
    _login(client, "pat_dis2")
    with app.app_context():
        r = Reminder(
            patient_id=pat_id,
            type="reassessment",
            message="Time for reassessment",
            status="pending",
        )
        db.session.add(r)
        db.session.commit()
        rid = r.id
    path = f"/reminders/{rid}/dismiss"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    with app.app_context():
        r = db.session.get(Reminder, rid)
        assert r.status == "dismissed"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# New-device / new-IP anomaly alerts
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _register_and_login_twice(app, client, username, ip1, ua1, ip2, ua2):
    """Helper: create user, first login from ip1/ua1, second from ip2/ua2."""
    _create_user(app, username, role="patient")
    # First login
    client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
        environ_base={"REMOTE_ADDR": ip1},
        headers={"User-Agent": ua1},
    )
    # Second login
    client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
        environ_base={"REMOTE_ADDR": ip2},
        headers={"User-Agent": ua2},
    )


def test_new_ip_alert_created_on_login(client, app, db):
    """Logging in from a new IP after a prior login triggers a new_ip alert."""
    from app.models.audit import AnomalyAlert
    username = "alert_ip_user1"
    _register_and_login_twice(
        app, client, username,
        ip1="10.0.0.1", ua1="Mozilla/5.0 Browser/1.0",
        ip2="192.168.99.1", ua2="Mozilla/5.0 Browser/1.0",
    )
    with app.app_context():
        alert = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
            AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_ip",
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains("192.168.99.1"),
        ).first()
        assert alert is not None
        assert alert.severity == "warning"


def test_new_device_alert_created_on_login(client, app, db):
    """Logging in from a new user-agent after a prior login triggers a new_device alert."""
    from app.models.audit import AnomalyAlert
    username = "alert_dev_user1"
    _register_and_login_twice(
        app, client, username,
        ip1="10.0.0.1", ua1="Chrome/100",
        ip2="10.0.0.1", ua2="Firefox/99",
    )
    with app.app_context():
        alert = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
            AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_device",
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
        ).first()
        assert alert is not None
        assert alert.severity == "info"


def test_no_alert_on_first_login(client, app, db):
    """First ever login for a user must not create a new_ip or new_device alert."""
    from app.models.audit import AnomalyAlert
    username = "first_login_user"
    _create_user(app, username, role="patient")
    client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
        environ_base={"REMOTE_ADDR": "10.1.2.3"},
        headers={"User-Agent": "SomeAgent/1.0"},
    )
    with app.app_context():
        alerts = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
        ).all()
        assert len(alerts) == 0


def test_no_alert_on_known_ip_and_device(client, app, db):
    """Repeated login from the same IP and UA does not create duplicate alerts."""
    from app.models.audit import AnomalyAlert
    username = "repeat_login_user"
    same_ip = "10.5.5.5"
    same_ua = "KnownBrowser/2.0"
    _create_user(app, username, role="patient")
    for _ in range(3):
        client.post(
            "/auth/login",
            data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
            environ_base={"REMOTE_ADDR": same_ip},
            headers={"User-Agent": same_ua},
        )
    with app.app_context():
        count = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
        ).count()
        assert count == 0


def test_new_ip_alert_deduplication(client, app, db):
    """A second login from the same new IP within 24h must not create a duplicate alert."""
    from app.models.audit import AnomalyAlert
    username = "dedup_ip_user"
    _create_user(app, username, role="patient")
    # Establish a known login
    client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
        environ_base={"REMOTE_ADDR": "10.0.0.1"},
        headers={"User-Agent": "BrowserA/1"},
    )
    new_ip = "172.16.0.1"
    # Two logins from the new IP in quick succession
    for _ in range(2):
        client.post(
            "/auth/login",
            data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
            environ_base={"REMOTE_ADDR": new_ip},
            headers={"User-Agent": "BrowserA/1"},
        )
    with app.app_context():
        count = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
            AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_ip",
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains(new_ip),
        ).count()
        assert count == 1  # deduplicated — only one alert despite two logins


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Health detailed gated to administrators
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_health_detailed_requires_auth(client, app, db):
    """/health/detailed must not be accessible without authentication."""
    resp = client.get("/health/detailed")
    assert resp.status_code in (302, 401, 403)


def test_health_detailed_accessible_to_admin(client, app, db):
    """/health/detailed must be accessible to administrators."""
    _create_user(app, "admin_health1", role="administrator")
    _login(client, "admin_health1")
    resp = client.get("/health/detailed")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.get_json()
    assert data["status"] == "ok"
    assert "database" in data


def test_health_detailed_forbidden_for_patient(client, app, db):
    """/health/detailed must not be accessible to patients."""
    _create_user(app, "patient_health1", role="patient")
    _login(client, "patient_health1")
    resp = client.get("/health/detailed")
    assert resp.status_code in (302, 403)"}

    """Perform a single isolated login using a fresh test client (avoids session carry-over)."""

            data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
            environ_base={"REMOTE_ADDR": ip},
            headers={"User-Agent": ua},

def _register_and_login_twice(app, client, username, ip1, ua1, ip2, ua2):
    """Helper: create user, first login from ip1/ua1, second from ip2/ua2.

    Uses separate test clients per login so the auth session from the first
    login doesn't short-circuit the second login's ``is_authenticated`` check.
    """
    _create_user(app, username, role="patient")
    _do_login(app, username, ip1, ua1)
    _do_login(app, username, ip2, ua2)"}

    """Perform a single isolated login using a fresh test client (avoids session carry-over).

    Uses environ_overrides (not environ_base) so REMOTE_ADDR is never shadowed
    by Werkzeug's built-in default of 127.0.0.1.
    """
    with app.test_client() as c:
        c.post(
            "/auth/login",
            data={"username": username, "password": "Password1"},
            environ_overrides={"REMOTE_ADDR": ip},
            headers={"User-Agent": ua},
        )"}

def _register_and_login_twice(app, client, username, ip1, ua1, ip2, ua2):
    """Helper: create user, first login from ip1/ua1, second from ip2/ua2.

    Uses separate test clients per login so the auth session from the first
    login doesn't short-circuit the second login's ``is_authenticated`` check.
    """
    _create_user(app, username, role="patient")
    _do_login(app, username, ip1, ua1)
    _do_login(app, username, ip2, ua2)


def test_new_ip_alert_created_on_login(client, app, db):
    """Logging in from a new IP after a prior login triggers a new_ip alert."""
    from app.models.audit import AnomalyAlert
    username = "alert_ip_user1"
    _register_and_login_twice(
        app, client, username,
        ip1="10.0.0.1", ua1="Mozilla/5.0 Browser/1.0",
        ip2="192.168.99.1", ua2="Mozilla/5.0 Browser/1.0",
    )
    with app.app_context():
        alert = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
            AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_ip",
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
            AnomalyAlert.message.contains("192.168.99.1"),
        ).first()

    """Perform a single isolated login using a fresh test client (avoids session carry-over).

All 233 tests pass. Here's the complete report:

---

## Changed files and rationale

### 1. Visit transition idempotency wiring

**`app/templates/visits/_visit_rows.html`**
Added `<input type="hidden" name="request_token" value="{{ generate_request_token() }}">` to every transition form. Each server-rendered form gets a UUID that's stable for the lifetime of that render — duplicate clicks submit the same token, which the state machine deduplicates.

**`app/routes/visits.py`**
Added server-side enforcement: if `request_token` is empty, returns HTTP 422 (HTMX) or redirect with flash (full-page). The token is always a non-empty string before being passed to `transition_visit`.

### 2. Anti-replay coverage gaps

**`app/routes/schedule.py`** — Added `@antireplay` to `holidays` and `bulk_generate` routes.

**`app/templates/schedule/holidays.html`** — Added `{{ antireplay_inputs("POST", _add_action) }}` to the add-holiday form.

**`app/templates/schedule/bulk_generate.html`** — Added `{{ antireplay_inputs("POST", _gen_action) }}` to the generate form.

**`app/routes/reminders.py`** — Added `@antireplay` to `dismiss` (patient-facing state mutation, consistent with `hold`/`confirm`/`cancel`).

**`app/templates/reminders/list.html`** — Added `{{ antireplay_inputs("POST", _dismiss_action) }}` to each dismiss form.

### 3. New-device/IP anomaly alerts

**`app/utils/audit.py`** — Added `check_new_device_alert(user_id, username, ip, ua)`. Queries prior successful `LoginAttempt` records, compares current IP/UA to known set, creates `AnomalyAlert` rows with `alert_type="new_ip"` (warning) or `"new_device"` (info). 24-hour cooldown deduplication per user+IP and user+device. Skips on first-ever login. All exceptions are caught and rolled back — never blocks login.

**`app/routes/auth.py`** — Imported `check_new_device_alert` and called it after `_record_attempt` in the success branch, wrapped in `try/except` so it never blocks login.

### 4. Health diagnostics gating (optional)

**`app/routes/health.py`** — Replaced `@csrf.exempt` on `/health/detailed` with `@role_required("administrator")`. The lightweight `/health` endpoint (used by Docker healthcheck) is unchanged.

### Updated tests

- **`tests/test_visits.py`** — Added `request_token` to `test_transition_endpoint`; added `test_transition_requires_request_token` (422 on missing token) and `test_transition_duplicate_token_is_idempotent` (same token twice = single transition).
- **`tests/test_audit_security.py`** — Added tests for holidays POST antireplay, bulk-generate antireplay, dismiss antireplay; direct unit tests for `check_new_device_alert` (new IP, new device, first login, known device, dedup); `/health/detailed` access control. Fixed `test_bulk_generate_skips_room_conflict_slots` to use `signed_data()`.
- **`tests/test_reminders.py`** — Updated `test_dismiss_reminder` and `test_dismiss_other_user_reminder_denied` to use `signed_data()`.
- **`tests/test_scheduling.py`** — Updated `test_holiday_blocks_slots` and `test_bulk_generate` to use `signed_data()`.
- **`tests/test_observability.py`** — Replaced the three "health/detailed is public" tests with tests that verify admin-only access.

**Result: 233 passed, 0 failed.**

Implement targeted fixes for the MeridianCare Flask project to close acceptance gaps found in audit. Do not redesign unrelated areas.
Scope:
1) Add encrypted clinical notes support
2) Ensure admin privileged mutations are fully audited with reason capture
3) Enforce ownership/object validation for assessment visit_id binding
4) Add/adjust tests for the above (non-Docker tests)
Requirements:
- Clinical notes
  - Add a persistent clinical notes model (patient-linked, author-linked, timestamps).
  - Encrypt note content at rest using existing ENCRYPTION_KEY utilities (app/utils/encryption.py).
  - Only decrypt at render time for authorized users.
  - Add role/object authorization:
    - Patient can read only own notes (if business flow allows patient visibility; otherwise explicitly deny).
    - Clinician/front desk/admin access according to existing RBAC patterns; no cross-patient leakage.
  - Add masking/safe output handling to avoid accidental sensitive exposure in logs/responses.
- Audit completeness for admin mutations
  - For app/routes/admin.py role change and status change endpoints:
    - Require a human-readable reason input for mutation requests.
    - Persist audit entries including: actor (who), action (what), target user, before/after values, timestamp (when), and reason (why).
  - Keep existing behavior protections (cannot self-demote, cannot demote last admin, etc.).
- Assessment visit_id validation
  - In assessment submit flow (app/routes/assessments.py):
    - If visit_id is provided, verify it exists and is authorized for current user context before writing AssessmentResult.
    - Patient users must only bind to their own visit.
    - Return clear validation errors on invalid/unauthorized visit_id (no silent accept).
- Tests (mandatory)
  - Add/extend tests to prove:
    1. Clinical notes are stored encrypted (not plaintext in DB) and authorization is enforced.
    2. Admin role/status changes fail without reason and create audit records with before/after + reason on success.
    3. Assessment submit rejects foreign/nonexistent visit_id and accepts authorized one.
  - Run: python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e/ -v
  - Do not run Docker commands.
Implementation constraints:
- Keep project conventions and module structure.
- No breaking changes to existing passing functionality.
- Prefer minimal, focused edits.
- Include migrations if schema changes are needed.
- Update README briefly for new clinical-notes and reason requirement behavior.

text.text:
Perfect! I have successfully read all the requested files. Here's a complete summary of what I found:

## File Contents Summary

### 1. **app/routes/admin.py** (Full)
Routes for user management with RBAC enforcement:
- `/admin/users` - list all users (requires administrator role)
- `/admin/users/<user_id>/role` - change user role with validation (prevents demoting last admin, self-demotion)
- `/admin/users/<user_id>/status` - activate/deactivate users (prevents self-deactivation)
- All routes use `@role_required("administrator")` and `@antireplay` decorators
- HTMX support for inline updates returning HTML fragments

### 2. **app/routes/assessments.py** (Full)
Assessment wizard routes:
- `/assessments/start` - begin assessment, load/create draft
- `/assessments/step/<step>` - handle step submission, save draft
- `/assessments/submit` - finalize assessment with idempotency check using request_token
- `/assessments/result/<assessment_id>` - view results with role-based access control
- `/assessments/history` - patient's own assessment history
- `/assessments/patient/<patient_id>` - staff view of patient assessments
- `/assessments/save-draft` - AJAX draft save

### 3. **app/models/assessment.py** (Full)
Three SQLAlchemy models:
- **AssessmentTemplate**: Assessment templates with questions_json and scoring_rules_json
- **AssessmentResult**: Stores completed assessments with answers_json, scores_json, risk_level, explanation_snapshot_json, and request_token for idempotency
- **AssessmentDraft**: In-progress assessments with partial_answers_json and current_step

### 4. **app/models/ directory listing**:
- `__init__.py`
- `__pycache__/`
- `assessment.py`
- `audit.py`
- `coverage.py`
- `demographics.py`
- `idempotency.py`
- `reminder.py`
- `scheduling.py`
- `user.py`
- `visit.py`

### 5. **app/utils/audit.py** (Full)
Audit and anomaly detection functions:
- `log_action()` - logs audit events with user, IP, user-agent
- `check_new_device_alert()` - creates anomaly alerts for new IPs/devices with 24-hour cooldown deduplication
- `anomaly_detection()` - detects >5 failed login attempts in 10 minutes

### 6. **app/utils/encryption.py** (Full)
Cryptographic utilities using Fernet:
- `_get_fernet()` - lazy-loads encryption cipher from ENCRYPTION_KEY config
- `encrypt_value()`/`decrypt_value()` - encrypt/decrypt strings
- `mask_id()` - mask values showing only last 4 chars
- `mask_encrypted_id()` - decrypt then mask
- `reset_fernet()` - for testing

### 7. **app/models/visit.py** (Full)
Two models:
- **Visit**: Visit records with patient_id, clinician_id, slot_id, status (booked/pending_payment/checked_in/seen/canceled/no_show)
- **VisitTransition**: Audit trail of status changes with request_token for idempotency

### 8. **app/models/user.py** (Full)
User models:
- **User**: UserMixin model with username, password_hash (bcrypt), role (administrator/clinician/front_desk/patient), is_active flag
- **LoginAttempt**: Records login attempts with username, IP, user-agent, success flag, timestamp

### 9. **migrations/ directory listing**:
- Empty directory with only `__init__.py`

### 10. **tests/test_rbac.py** (Full)
Comprehensive RBAC tests:
- Admin users page access control
- Role change with validation (can't change own, can't demote last admin)
- User deactivation and login prevention
- Invalid role rejection
- 403 page styling
- HTMX integration returning JSON errors
- Navigation link visibility based on role

### 11. **tests/test_assessments.py** (Full)
Assessment workflow tests:
- Scoring calculations for PHQ-9, GAD-7, blood pressure, fall risk, med adherence
- Risk level determination (Low/Moderate/High)
- Full wizard flow with draft saving
- Idempotency via request_token
- Access control (patients see own, staff see assigned)
- Draft persistence across steps

### 12. **app/templates/admin/_user_row.html** (Full)
User management row template with:
- Inline role selector with HTMX POST to change_role
- Status badge (Active/Inactive)
- Deactivate/Activate button with anti-replay signing
- Self-action prevention (role change, deactivation disabled for current user)

### 13. **app/templates/assessments/** (6 files):

**_review.html**: Review page showing all answers before submission with antireplay protection

**_step.html**: Individual step form with:
- Question display based on type (scale_0_3, yes_no, select)
- Radio buttons/dropdowns with saved answer restoration
- Hidden fields carry forward previous answers
- Next/Review button logic based on step count

**history.html**: Patient's assessment history with table showing Date, Risk Level, PHQ-9, GAD-7 scores

**patient_history.html**: Staff view of patient's assessments (same layout as history.html)

**result.html**: Assessment result display with:
- Overall risk level badge (color-coded)
- Individual score cards for PHQ-9, GAD-7, BP, Fall Risk, Med Adherence
- Expandable "Why this rating?" section with explanations
- Submission timestamp

**wizard.html**: Main assessment container with:
- Progress bar
- Step counter
- Includes _step.html template

### 14. **README.md (lines 1-30)**:
MeridianCare Clinic Operations Platform documentation covering:
- Python 3.10+ and Docker requirements
- Required environment variables (SECRET_KEY, ENCRYPTION_KEY, REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET)
- Quick start instructions with pip and Flask development setup

---

All files are now available for review. The codebase demonstrates a secure healthcare clinic platform with RBAC, audit logging, encryption, anti-replay protection, idempotency tokens, and comprehensive assessment management.

content.text: 1 import json 2 import uuid 3 from flask import Blueprint, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, flash, jsonify 4 from flask_login import login_required, current_user 5 from app.extensions import db 6 from app.models.assessment import AssessmentTemplate, AssessmentResult, AssessmentDraft 7 from app.utils.scoring import calculate_scores, calculate_risk_level, get_or_create_default_template 8 from app.utils.auth import role_required 9 from app.utils.antireplay import antireplay 10 11 assessments_bp = Blueprint("assessments", __name__, url_prefix="/assessments") 12 13 TOTAL_STEPS = 5 # PHQ-9, GAD-7, BP, Fall Risk, Med Adherence 14 15 16 @assessments_bp.route("/start") 17 @assessments_bp.route("/start/") 18 @login_required 19 def start(visit_id=None): 20 template = get_or_create_default_template(db.session) 21 sections = template.questions 22 23 # Check for existing draft 24 draft = AssessmentDraft.query.filter_by( 25 patient_id=current_user.id, 26 visit_id=visit_id, 27 template_id=template.id, 28 ).first() 29 30 current_step = draft.current_step if draft else 0 31 saved_answers = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) if draft else {} 32 request_token = str(uuid.uuid4()) 33 34 return render_template( 35 "assessments/wizard.html", 36 template=template, 37 sections=sections, 38 section=sections[current_step], 39 step=current_step, 40 current_step=current_step, 41 total_steps=TOTAL_STEPS, 42 visit_id=visit_id, 43 saved_answers=saved_answers, 44 request_token=request_token, 45 ) 46 47 48 @assessments_bp.route("/step/", methods=["POST"]) 49 @login_required 50 def wizard_step(step): 51 template = get_or_create_default_template(db.session) 52 sections = template.questions 53 visit_id = request.form.get("visit_id", type=int) 54 55 # Collect all answers from form 56 answers = {} 57 for key, val in request.form.items(): 58 if key not in ("csrf_token", "visit_id", "request_token", "step"): 59 answers[key] = val 60 61 # Save draft 62 draft = AssessmentDraft.query.filter_by( 63 patient_id=current_user.id, 64 visit_id=visit_id, 65 template_id=template.id, 66 ).first() 67 68 if draft: 69 existing = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) 70 existing.update(answers) 71 draft.partial_answers_json = json.dumps(existing) 72 draft.current_step = step 73 else: 74 draft = AssessmentDraft( 75 patient_id=current_user.id, 76 visit_id=visit_id, 77 template_id=template.id, 78 partial_answers_json=json.dumps(answers), 79 current_step=step, 80 ) 81 db.session.add(draft) 82 db.session.commit() 83 84 saved_answers = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) 85 request_token = request.form.get("request_token", str(uuid.uuid4())) 86 87 if step >= TOTAL_STEPS: 88 # Show review page 89 return render_template( 90 "assessments/_review.html", 91 template=template, 92 sections=sections, 93 answers=saved_answers, 94 visit_id=visit_id, 95 request_token=request_token, 96 ) 97 98 return render_template( 99 "assessments/_step.html", 100 template=template, 101 section=sections[step], 102 step=step, 103 total_steps=TOTAL_STEPS, 104 visit_id=visit_id, 105 saved_answers=saved_answers, 106 request_token=request_token, 107 ) 108 109 110 @assessments_bp.route("/submit", methods=["POST"]) 111 @login_required 112 @antireplay 113 def submit(): 114 template = get_or_create_default_template(db.session) 115 visit_id = request.form.get("visit_id", type=int) 116 request_token = request.form.get("request_token", "") 117 118 # Idempotency check 119 if request_token: 120 existing = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(request_token=request_token).first() 121 if existing: 122 return redirect(url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=existing.id)) 123 124 # Get answers from draft 125 draft = AssessmentDraft.query.filter_by( 126 patient_id=current_user.id, 127 visit_id=visit_id, 128 template_id=template.id, 129 ).first() 130 131 if not draft: 132 flash("No assessment data found. Please start again.", "danger") 133 return redirect(url_for("assessments.start", visit_id=visit_id)) 134 135 answers = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) 136 scores = calculate_scores(answers) 137 risk_level, explanations = calculate_risk_level(scores) 138 139 result = AssessmentResult( 140 patient_id=current_user.id, 141 visit_id=visit_id, 142 template_id=template.id, 143 template_version=template.version, 144 answers_json=json.dumps(answers), 145 scores_json=json.dumps(scores), 146 risk_level=risk_level, 147 explanation_snapshot_json=json.dumps(explanations), 148 request_token=request_token or None, 149 ) 150 db.session.add(result) 151 db.session.delete(draft) 152 db.session.commit() 153 154 if request.headers.get("HX-Request"): 155 resp = jsonify({"redirect": url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=result.id)}) 156 resp.headers["HX-Redirect"] = url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=result.id) 157 return resp 158 return redirect(url_for("assessments.result", assessment_id=result.id)) 159 160 161 @assessments_bp.route("/result/") 162 @login_required 163 def result(assessment_id): 164 assessment = db.session.get(AssessmentResult, assessment_id) 165 if not assessment: 166 flash("Assessment not found.", "danger") 167 return redirect(url_for("assessments.history")) 168 169 # Access check: patient can see own, staff can see assigned 170 if current_user.role == "patient" and assessment.patient_id != current_user.id: 171 flash("Access denied.", "danger") 172 return redirect(url_for("assessments.history")) 173 174 return render_template( 175 "assessments/result.html", 176 assessment=assessment, 177 scores=assessment.scores, 178 explanations=assessment.explanation, 179 ) 180 181 182 @assessments_bp.route("/history") 183 @login_required 184 def history(): 185 if current_user.role == "patient": 186 results = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by( 187 patient_id=current_user.id 188 ).order_by(AssessmentResult.submitted_at.desc()).all() 189 else: 190 results = [] 191 return render_template("assessments/history.html", results=results) 192 193 194 @assessments_bp.route("/save-draft", methods=["POST"]) 195 @login_required 196 def save_draft(): 197 template = get_or_create_default_template(db.session) 198 visit_id = request.form.get("visit_id", type=int) 199 answers = {} 200 for key, val in request.form.items(): 201 if key not in ("csrf_token", "visit_id", "request_token", "step"): 202 answers[key] = val 203 204 draft = AssessmentDraft.query.filter_by( 205 patient_id=current_user.id, 206 visit_id=visit_id, 207 template_id=template.id, 208 ).first() 209 210 if draft: 211 existing = json.loads(draft.partial_answers_json) 212 existing.update(answers) 213 draft.partial_answers_json = json.dumps(existing) 214 else: 215 draft = AssessmentDraft( 216 patient_id=current_user.id, 217 visit_id=visit_id, 218 template_id=template.id, 219 partial_answers_json=json.dumps(answers), 220 ) 221 db.session.add(draft) 222 db.session.commit() 223 return 'Draft saved' 224 225 226 # Staff view of patient assessments 227 @assessments_bp.route("/patient/") 228 @role_required("administrator", "clinician", "front_desk") 229 def patient_assessments(patient_id): 230 results = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by( 231 patient_id=patient_id 232 ).order_by(AssessmentResult.submitted_at.desc()).all() 233 from app.models.user import User 234 patient = db.session.get(User, patient_id) 235 return render_template("assessments/patient_history.html", results=results, patient=patient) 236 Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1	import json
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone
3	from app.extensions import db
4	
5	
6	class AssessmentTemplate(db.Model):
7	    __tablename__ = "assessment_templates"
8	
9	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
10	    name = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
11	    version = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False, default=1)
12	    questions_json = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
13	    scoring_rules_json = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
14	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
15	
16	    __table_args__ = (db.UniqueConstraint("name", "version"),)
17	
18	    @property
19	    def questions(self):
20	        return json.loads(self.questions_json)
21	
22	    @property
23	    def scoring_rules(self):
24	        return json.loads(self.scoring_rules_json)
25	
26	
27	class AssessmentResult(db.Model):
28	    __tablename__ = "assessment_results"
29	
30	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
31	    patient_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True)
32	    visit_id = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=True, index=True)
33	    template_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("assessment_templates.id"), nullable=False)
34	    template_version = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)
35	    answers_json = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
36	    scores_json = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
37	    risk_level = db.Column(db.String(20), nullable=False)  # Low, Moderate, High
38	    explanation_snapshot_json = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
39	    request_token = db.Column(db.String(64), unique=True, nullable=True)
40	    submitted_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
41	
42	    patient = db.relationship("User", foreign_keys=[patient_id])
43	    template = db.relationship("AssessmentTemplate")
44	
45	    @property
46	    def answers(self):
47	        return json.loads(self.answers_json)
48	
49	    @property
50	    def scores(self):
51	        return json.loads(self.scores_json)
52	
53	    @property

56

68	        db.DateTime,

1	"""Tests for prompt 03 — RBAC authorization."""
2	
3	import pytest
4	from app.models.user import User
5	from app.extensions import db
6	from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data
7	
8	
9	def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
10	    with app.app_context():
11	        user = User(username=username, role=role)
12	        user.set_password(password)
13	        db.session.add(user)
14	        db.session.commit()
15	        return user.id
16	
17	
18	def _login(client, username="testuser", password="Password1"):
19	    return client.post(
20	        "/auth/login",
21	        data={"username": username, "password": password},
22	        follow_redirects=True,
23	    )
24	
25	
26	def test_admin_users_page_requires_login(client):
27	    resp = client.get("/admin/users", follow_redirects=True)
28	    assert b"Log In" in resp.data
29	
30	
31	def test_admin_users_page_requires_admin(client, app):
32	    _create_user(app, "patient1", role="patient")
33	    _login(client, "patient1")
34	    resp = client.get("/admin/users")
35	    assert resp.status_code == 403
36	
37	
38	def test_admin_users_page_accessible_by_admin(client, app):
39	    _create_user(app, "admin1", role="administrator")
40	    _login(client, "admin1")
41	    resp = client.get("/admin/users")
42	    assert resp.status_code == 200
43	    assert b"User Management" in resp.data
44	
45	
46	def test_clinician_cannot_access_admin(client, app):
47	    _create_user(app, "clinician1", role="clinician")
48	    _login(client, "clinician1")
49	    resp = client.get("/admin/users")
50	    assert resp.status_code == 403
51	
52	
53	def test_front_desk_cannot_access_admin(client, app):
54	    _create_user(app, "frontdesk1", role="front_desk")
55	    _login(client, "frontdesk1")
56	    resp = client.get("/admin/users")
57	    assert resp.status_code == 403
58	
59	
60	def test_change_user_role(client, app):
61	    _create_user(app, "admin2", role="administrator")
62	    uid = _create_user(app, "user2", role="patient")
63	    _login(client, "admin2")
64	    path = f"/admin/users/{uid}/role"
65	    resp = client.post(
66	        path,
67	        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"role": "clinician"}),
68	        headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
69	    )
70	    assert resp.status_code == 200
71	    with app.app_context():
72	        user = db.session.get(User, uid)
73	        assert user.role == "clinician"
74	
75	
76	def test_cannot_change_own_role(client, app):
77	    _create_user(app, "admin3", role="administrator")
78	    _login(client, "admin3")
79	    with app.app_context():
80	        admin = User.query.filter_by(username="admin3").first()
81	        resp = client.post(
82	            f"/admin/users/{admin.id}/role",
83	            data={"role": "patient"},
84	            headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
85	        )
86	        assert resp.status_code == 400
87	
88	
89	def test_cannot_demote_last_admin(client, app):
90	    uid = _create_user(app, "admin4", role="administrator")
91	    _create_user(app, "admin5", role="administrator")

{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\app\models\clinical_note.py", "content": "from datetime import datetime, timezone
from app.extensions import db
from app.utils.encryption import encrypt_value, decrypt_value


class ClinicalNote(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "clinical_notes"

    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    patient_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True)
    author_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True)
    visit_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("visits.id"), nullable=True)
    content_encrypted = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updated_at = db.Column(
        db.DateTime,
        default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        onupdate=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )

    patient = db.relationship("User", foreign_keys=[patient_id])
    author = db.relationship("User", foreign_keys=[author_id])
    visit = db.relationship("Visit", backref="clinical_notes")

    @property
    def content(self):
        """Decrypt and return note content. Call only in an authorized render context."""
        return decrypt_value(self.content_encrypted) or ""

    @classmethod
    def create(cls, patient_id, author_id, content, visit_id=None):
        """Encrypt content and return an unsaved ClinicalNote instance."""
        return cls(
            patient_id=patient_id,
            author_id=author_id,
            visit_id=visit_id,
            content_encrypted=encrypt_value(content),
        )
"}

  ```python
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from app.extensions import db
from app.utils.encryption import encrypt_value, decrypt_value

class ClinicalNote(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = "clinical_notes"
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    patient_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True)
    author_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True)
    visit_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("visits.id"), nullable=True)
    content_encrypted = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False)
    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updated_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc),
                           onupdate=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    patient = db.relationship("User", foreign_keys=[patient_id])
    author = db.relationship("User", foreign_keys=[author_id])
    visit = db.relationship("Visit", backref="clinical_notes")

    @property
    def content(self):
        return decrypt_value(self.content_encrypted) or ""

    @classmethod
    def create(cls, patient_id, author_id, content, visit_id=None):
        return cls(patient_id=patient_id, author_id=author_id,
                   visit_id=visit_id, content_encrypted=encrypt_value(content))
```

1	import atexit
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from flask import Flask
5	from app.config import config_by_name
6	from app.extensions import db, csrf, migrate, login_manager
7	from app.utils.logging import setup_logging
8	from app.utils.middleware import register_middleware
9	
10	
11	def _start_reminder_scheduler(app):
12	    """Start a background thread that runs reminder generation every 15 minutes.
13	
14	    Guards:
15	    - Skipped entirely in testing mode.
16	    - In debug mode with the Werkzeug reloader, only the worker subprocess starts
17	      the scheduler (avoids double-scheduling in the monitor/reloader process).
18	    - Registered with atexit for graceful shutdown.
19	    """
20	    try:
21	        from apscheduler.schedulers.background import BackgroundScheduler
22	    except ImportError:
23	        app.logger.warning(
24	            "APScheduler is not installed — background reminder generation disabled. "
25	            "Run: pip install APScheduler>=3.10,<4.0"
26	        )
27	        return None
28	    from app.utils.reminders import generate_pending_reminders
29	
30	    scheduler = BackgroundScheduler(daemon=True)
31	
32	    def _job():
33	        with app.app_context():
34	            try:
35	                generate_pending_reminders()
36	            except Exception:
37	                pass  # never let a DB error crash the scheduler thread
38	
39	    scheduler.add_job(_job, "interval", minutes=15, id="reminder_generation")
40	    scheduler.start()
41	    atexit.register(lambda: scheduler.shutdown(wait=False))
42	    return scheduler
43	
44	
45	def create_app(config_name=None):
46	    if config_name is None:
47	        config_name = os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "production")
48	
49	    cfg = config_by_name[config_name]
50	    if hasattr(cfg, "validate"):
51	        cfg.validate()
52	
53	    app = Flask(__name__)
54	    app.config.from_object(cfg)
55	
56	    # Initialize extensions
57	    db.init_app(app)
58	    csrf.init_app(app)
59	    migrate.init_app(app, db)
60	    login_manager.init_app(app)
61	
62	    # Setup logging and middleware
63	    setup_logging(app)
64	    register_middleware(app)
65	
66	    # Register blueprints
67	    from app.routes.main import main_bp
68	    from app.routes.health import health_bp
69	    from app.routes.auth import auth_bp
70	    from app.routes.admin import admin_bp
71	    from app.routes.patient import patient_bp
72	    from app.routes.staff import staff_bp
73	    from app.routes.assessments import assessments_bp
74	    from app.routes.schedule import schedule_bp
75	    from app.routes.visits import visits_bp
76	    from app.routes.coverage import coverage_bp
77	    from app.routes.audit import audit_bp
78	    from app.routes.reminders import reminders_bp
79	    from app.routes.observability import observability_bp
80	
81	    app.register_blueprint(main_bp)
82	    app.register_blueprint(health_bp)
83	    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp)
84	    app.register_blueprint(admin_bp)
85	    app.register_blueprint(patient_bp)
86	    app.register_blueprint(staff_bp)
87	    app.register_blueprint(assessments_bp)
88	    app.register_blueprint(schedule_bp)
89	    app.register_blueprint(visits_bp)
90	    app.register_blueprint(coverage_bp)
91	    app.register_blueprint(audit_bp)
92	    app.register_blueprint(reminders_bp)
93	    app.register_blueprint(observability_bp)
94	
95	    # Template helpers
96	    @app.context_processor
97	    def inject_request_token():
98	        def request_token():
99	            token = str(uuid.uuid4())
100	            return token
101	        return dict(generate_request_token=request_token)
102	
103	    @app.context_processor
104	    def inject_antireplay_helpers():
105	        import hmac as _hmac
106	        import hashlib as _hashlib
107	        from datetime import datetime, timezone as _tz
108	        from markupsafe import Markup
109	
110	        def antireplay_inputs(method, path):
111	            """Render three hidden fields: _nonce, _timestamp, _signature.
112	
113	            The signature is HMAC-SHA256 over 'METHOD|path|nonce|timestamp'
114	            using REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET.  All values are server-generated so
115	            the secret never reaches the browser.
116	            """
117	            nonce = str(uuid.uuid4())
118	            timestamp = datetime.now(_tz.utc).isoformat()
119	            secret = app.config.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "")
120	            payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
121	            sig = _hmac.new(secret.encode(), payload.encode(), _hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
122	            return Markup(
123	                f'<input type="hidden" name="_nonce" value="{nonce}">'
124	                f'<input type="hidden" name="_timestamp" value="{timestamp}">'
125	                f'<input type="hidden" name="_signature" value="{sig}">'
126	            )
127	
128	        # Kept for any template that still calls them individually.
129	        def generate_nonce():
130	            return str(uuid.uuid4())
131	
132	        def generate_timestamp():
133	            return datetime.now(_tz.utc).isoformat()
134	
135	        return dict(
136	            antireplay_inputs=antireplay_inputs,
137	            generate_nonce=generate_nonce,
138	            generate_timestamp=generate_timestamp,
139	        )
140	
141	    # Error handlers
142	    from flask import render_template, jsonify, request as flask_request
143	
144	    @app.errorhandler(403)
145	    def forbidden(e):
146	        if flask_request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
147	            return jsonify({"error": "Access denied"}), 403
148	        return render_template("errors/403.html"), 403
149	
150	    @app.errorhandler(404)
151	    def not_found(e):
152	        if flask_request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
153	            return jsonify({"error": "Not found"}), 404
154	        return render_template("errors/404.html"), 404
155	
156	    # Create database tables
157	    with app.app_context():
158	        from app import models  # noqa: F401
159	        db.create_all()
160	
161	    # Start in-process reminder scheduler (skipped in testing and in the
162	    # Werkzeug reloader monitor process to avoid duplicate jobs).
163	    if not app.testing:
164	        from werkzeug.serving import is_running_from_reloader
165	        if not app.debug or is_running_from_reloader():
166	            _start_reminder_scheduler(app)
167	
168	    return app
169

89	def test_cannot_demote_last_admin(client, app):
90	    uid = _create_user(app, "admin4", role="administrator")
91	    _create_user(app, "admin5", role="administrator")

1	"""Shared test helpers for anti-replay signed request data.
2	
3	The secret must match TestingConfig.REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET exactly so that the
4	decorator's HMAC verification passes in test runs.
5	"""
6	import hmac
7	import hashlib
8	import uuid
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	
11	TEST_SIGNING_SECRET = "test-request-signing-secret-dev-only"
12	
13	
14	def signed_data(method: str, path: str, extra: dict | None = None) -> dict:
15	    """Return a form-data dict with _nonce, _timestamp, and _signature.
16	
17	    Parameters
18	    ----------
19	    method : HTTP verb (e.g. "POST")
20	    path   : URL path exactly as Flask will see it in request.path
21	             (e.g. "/visits/1/transition")
22	    extra  : additional form fields to merge in (e.g. {"target_state": "checked_in"})
23	    """
24	    nonce = str(uuid.uuid4())
25	    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
26	    payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
27	    sig = hmac.new(
28	        TEST_SIGNING_SECRET.encode(),
29	        payload.encode(),
30	        hashlib.sha256,
31	    ).hexdigest()
32	    data = {"_nonce": nonce, "_timestamp": timestamp, "_signature": sig}
33	    if extra:
34	        data.update(extra)
35	    return data
36

12	def log_action(action, resource_type, resource_id=None, details=None):
13	    """Log an audit event capturing the current user and request context."""
14	    user_id = None
15	    ip_address = None
16	    user_agent = None
17	
18	    if has_request_context():
19	        if current_user and hasattr(current_user, "id") and current_user.is_authenticated:
20	            user_id = current_user.id
21	        ip_address = request.headers.get("X-Forwarded-For", request.remote_addr) or "unknown"
22	        user_agent = (request.headers.get("User-Agent", "") or "")[:500]
23	
24	    entry = AuditLog(
25	        user_id=user_id,
26	        action=action,
27	        resource_type=resource_type,
28	        resource_id=str(resource_id) if resource_id is not None else None,
29	        details_json=details,
30	        ip_address=ip_address,
31	        user_agent=user_agent,
32	    )
33	    db.session.add(entry)
34	    db.session.commit()
35	    return entry
36	
37	
38	def check_new_device_alert(user_id, username, ip, ua):
39	    """Create AnomalyAlert when a user logs in from a new IP or new device.
40	
41	    A new IP/device is one not seen in any prior successful login for this user.
42	    Alerts are deduplicated within a 24-hour cooldown window per user+IP and
43	    user+device to prevent alert fatigue.  Called after _record_attempt so the
44	    current login is already persisted — we exclude the most-recent record.
45	    """
46	    try:
47	        # All successful logins for this username, oldest-first.
48	        # The last entry is the one just recorded (current login).
49	        all_ok = (
50	            LoginAttempt.query
51	            .filter_by(username=username, success=True)
52	            .order_by(LoginAttempt.attempted_at.asc())
53	            .all()
54	        )
55	        # If this is the very first login there are no prior sessions to compare.
56	        if len(all_ok) <= 1:
57	            return
58	
59	        prior = all_ok[:-1]  # everything before the current login
60	        known_ips = {a.ip_address for a in prior if a.ip_address}

1	import hmac
2	import hashlib
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
4	from flask import request, current_app, jsonify
5	from functools import wraps
6	from app.extensions import db
7	from app.models.audit import SignedRequest
8	
9	
10	def _hash_nonce(nonce: str) -> str:
11	    """One-way hash a nonce for safe at-rest storage."""
12	    return hashlib.sha256(nonce.encode()).hexdigest()
13	
14	REPLAY_WINDOW = timedelta(minutes=5)
15	
16	
17	def _compute_signature(secret: str, method: str, path: str, nonce: str, timestamp: str) -> str:
18	    """HMAC-SHA256 over 'METHOD|path|nonce|timestamp' using the server signing secret."""
19	    payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
20	    return hmac.new(secret.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
21	
22	
23	def antireplay(f):
24	    @wraps(f)
25	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
26	        # Only enforce anti-replay on state-mutating methods.
27	        if request.method not in ("POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE"):
28	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
29	
30	        # Lazily purge expired nonces so the table doesn't grow unbounded.
31	        try:
32	            SignedRequest.query.filter(
33	                SignedRequest.expires_at < datetime.now(timezone.utc)
34	            ).delete()
35	            db.session.commit()
36	        except Exception:
37	            db.session.rollback()
38	
39	        nonce = request.headers.get("X-Nonce") or request.form.get("_nonce")
40	        ts_header = request.headers.get("X-Timestamp") or request.form.get("_timestamp")
41	        signature = request.headers.get("X-Signature") or request.form.get("_signature")
42	
43	        if not nonce or not ts_header:
44	            return jsonify({"error": "Missing nonce or timestamp"}), 400
45	
46	        if not signature:
47	            return jsonify({"error": "Missing request signature"}), 400
48	
49	        try:
50	            ts = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_header)
51	            if ts.tzinfo is None:
52	                ts = ts.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
53	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
54	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
55	
56	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
57	        if abs(now - ts) > REPLAY_WINDOW:
58	            return jsonify({"error": "Request expired, please try again"}), 400
59	
60	        # Signature verification — must happen before the nonce is stored so
61	        # an attacker cannot use a 409 response to probe whether a nonce exists.
62	        secret = current_app.config.get("REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET", "")
63	        expected = _compute_signature(secret, request.method, request.path, nonce, ts_header)
64	        if not hmac.compare_digest(signature, expected):
65	            return jsonify({"error": "Invalid request signature"}), 400
66	
67	        # Replay check — nonce must not have been seen before.
68	        # Store and compare the SHA-256 hash so raw nonces never reach the DB.
69	        nonce_hash = _hash_nonce(nonce)
70	        existing = SignedRequest.query.filter_by(nonce=nonce_hash).first()
71	        if existing:
72	            return jsonify({"error": "Request already processed"}), 409
73	
74	        sr = SignedRequest(nonce=nonce_hash, timestamp=ts, expires_at=ts + REPLAY_WINDOW)
75	        db.session.add(sr)
76	        db.session.commit()
77	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
78	    return decorated
79

{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\tests\test_notes.py", "content": """"Tests for clinical notes — encryption, authorization, cross-patient isolation."""

import pytest
from app.models.user import User
from app.models.clinical_note import ClinicalNote
from app.extensions import db
from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data


def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role=role)
        user.set_password(password)
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        return user.id


def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Encryption at rest
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_note_content_encrypted_in_db(app):
    """content_encrypted must not equal the plaintext note."""
    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=1, author_id=1, content="Sensitive clinical finding"
        )
        assert note.content_encrypted != "Sensitive clinical finding"
        assert "Sensitive" not in note.content_encrypted


def test_note_decrypts_correctly(app):
    """content property must return the original plaintext."""
    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=1, author_id=1, content="Blood pressure normal"
        )
        assert note.content == "Blood pressure normal"


def test_note_stored_encrypted_not_plaintext(app):
    """After persisting to DB, the raw column value is ciphertext."""
    uid = _create_user(app, "clinician_enc", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_enc")
    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=pid, author_id=uid, content="My secret note"
        )
        db.session.add(note)
        db.session.commit()
        nid = note.id

    with app.app_context():
        from app.extensions import db as _db
        raw = _db.session.execute(
            _db.text("SELECT content_encrypted FROM clinical_notes WHERE id = :id"),
            {"id": nid},
        ).fetchone()[0]
        assert "My secret note" not in raw


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Staff can create and read notes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_clinician_can_create_note(client, app):
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_cr", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_cr")
    _login(client, "clinician_cr")

    path = f"/notes/patient/{pid}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Routine checkup note"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid).first()
        assert note is not None
        assert note.content == "Routine checkup note"


def test_admin_can_create_note(client, app):
    _create_user(app, "admin_note", role="administrator")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_adm")
    _login(client, "admin_note")

    path = f"/notes/patient/{pid}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Admin observation"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid).first()
        assert note is not None


def test_staff_can_read_patient_notes(client, app):
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_rd", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_rd")

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(patient_id=pid, author_id=cid, content="Lab results normal")
        db.session.add(note)
        db.session.commit()

    _login(client, "clinician_rd")
    resp = client.get(f"/notes/patient/{pid}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Lab results normal" in resp.data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Patient reads own notes only
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_patient_can_read_own_notes(client, app):
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_pat", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_own")

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(patient_id=pid, author_id=cid, content="Patient's own note")
        db.session.add(note)
        db.session.commit()

    _login(client, "patient_own")
    resp = client.get("/notes/my")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Patient's own note" in resp.data


def test_patient_cannot_access_staff_notes_route(client, app):
    pid1 = _create_user(app, "patient_x1")
    pid2 = _create_user(app, "patient_x2")
    _login(client, "patient_x1")

    resp = client.get(f"/notes/patient/{pid2}")
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_patient_my_notes_no_cross_leak(client, app):
    """A patient's /notes/my must not show another patient's notes."""
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_iso", role="clinician")
    pid1 = _create_user(app, "patient_iso1")
    pid2 = _create_user(app, "patient_iso2")

    with app.app_context():
        note_for_2 = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=pid2, author_id=cid, content="Private note for patient 2"
        )
        db.session.add(note_for_2)
        db.session.commit()

    _login(client, "patient_iso1")
    resp = client.get("/notes/my")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Private note for patient 2" not in resp.data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Authorization: patient cannot create notes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_patient_cannot_post_note(client, app):
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_nopost")
    _login(client, "patient_nopost")

    path = f"/notes/patient/{pid}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Self-written note"}),
        follow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_unauthenticated_cannot_read_notes(client, app):
    resp = client.get("/notes/my")
    assert resp.status_code in (302, 401)
"}

def test_note_content_encrypted_in_db(app):

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=1, author_id=1, content="Sensitive clinical finding"
        )
        assert note.content_encrypted != "Sensitive clinical finding"
        assert "Sensitive" not in note.content_encrypted


def test_note_decrypts_correctly(app):
    """content property must return the original plaintext."""
    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=1, author_id=1, content="Blood pressure normal"
        )
        assert note.content == "Blood pressure normal"


def test_note_stored_encrypted_not_plaintext(app):
    """After persisting to DB, the raw column value is ciphertext."""
    uid = _create_user(app, "clinician_enc", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_enc")
    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=pid, author_id=uid, content="My secret note"
        )
        db.session.add(note)
        db.session.commit()
        nid = note.id

    with app.app_context():
        from app.extensions import db as _db
        raw = _db.session.execute(
            _db.text("SELECT content_encrypted FROM clinical_notes WHERE id = :id"),
            {"id": nid},
        ).fetchone()[0]
        assert "My secret note" not in raw


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Staff can create and read notes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_clinician_can_create_note(client, app):
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_cr", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_cr")
    _login(client, "clinician_cr")

    path = f"/notes/patient/{pid}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Routine checkup note"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid).first()
        assert note is not None
        assert note.content == "Routine checkup note"


def test_admin_can_create_note(client, app):
    _create_user(app, "admin_note", role="administrator")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_adm")
    _login(client, "admin_note")

    path = f"/notes/patient/{pid}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Admin observation"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid).first()
        assert note is not None


def test_staff_can_read_patient_notes(client, app):
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_rd", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_rd")

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(patient_id=pid, author_id=cid, content="Lab results normal")
        db.session.add(note)
        db.session.commit()

    _login(client, "clinician_rd")
    resp = client.get(f"/notes/patient/{pid}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Lab results normal" in resp.data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Patient reads own notes only
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_patient_can_read_own_notes(client, app):
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_pat", role="clinician")
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_own")

    with app.app_context():
        note = ClinicalNote.create(patient_id=pid, author_id=cid, content="Patient's own note")
        db.session.add(note)
        db.session.commit()

    _login(client, "patient_own")
    resp = client.get("/notes/my")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Patient's own note" in resp.data


def test_patient_cannot_access_staff_notes_route(client, app):
    pid1 = _create_user(app, "patient_x1")
    pid2 = _create_user(app, "patient_x2")
    _login(client, "patient_x1")

    resp = client.get(f"/notes/patient/{pid2}")
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_patient_my_notes_no_cross_leak(client, app):
    """A patient's /notes/my must not show another patient's notes."""
    cid = _create_user(app, "clinician_iso", role="clinician")
    pid1 = _create_user(app, "patient_iso1")
    pid2 = _create_user(app, "patient_iso2")

    with app.app_context():
        note_for_2 = ClinicalNote.create(
            patient_id=pid2, author_id=cid, content="Private note for patient 2"
        )
        db.session.add(note_for_2)
        db.session.commit()

    _login(client, "patient_iso1")
    resp = client.get("/notes/my")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Private note for patient 2" not in resp.data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Authorization: patient cannot create notes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_patient_cannot_post_note(client, app):
    pid = _create_user(app, "patient_nopost")
    _login(client, "patient_nopost")

    path = f"/notes/patient/{pid}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Self-written note"}),
        follow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_unauthenticated_cannot_read_notes(client, app):
    resp = client.get("/notes/my")
    assert resp.status_code in (302, 401)

import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
from app.models.user import User
from app.models.visit import Visit
from app.models.scheduling import Clinician, Slot
from app.extensions import db
from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data


def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role=role)
        user.set_password(password)
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        return user.id


def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


def _create_visit(app, patient_id, clinician_id):
    """Create a Visit record and return its id."""
    with app.app_context():
        visit = Visit(patient_id=patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id, status="booked")
        db.session.add(visit)
        db.session.commit()
        return visit.id


def _create_clinician(app, username="doc_assess"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
        user.set_password("Password1")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        c = Clinician(user_id=user.id)
        db.session.add(c)
        db.session.commit()
        return c.id


def _start_and_fill_assessment(client, visit_id=None):
    """Run through wizard steps so a draft exists, then return review."""
    start_url = "/assessments/start"
    if visit_id:
        start_url = f"/assessments/start/{visit_id}"
    client.get(start_url)

    import uuid
    token = str(uuid.uuid4())
    for step in range(5):
        client.post(
            f"/assessments/step/{step}",
            data={"request_token": token, "visit_id": visit_id or "", "step": step},
        )
    return token


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# visit_id validation in submit()
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_submit_rejects_nonexistent_visit_id(client, app):
    """submit() must reject a visit_id that doesn't exist."""
    pid = _create_user(app, "pat_vv1")
    _login(client, "pat_vv1")

    token = _start_and_fill_assessment(client, visit_id=None)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {
            "request_token": token,
            "visit_id": "99999",
        }),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    # Should not complete successfully — must show error or redirect back
    assert b"Invalid visit" in resp.data or resp.status_code in (302, 400)


def test_submit_rejects_foreign_visit_id(client, app):
    """Patient A cannot bind an assessment to Patient B's visit."""
    cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_fv")
    pid_a = _create_user(app, "pat_fv_a")
    pid_b = _create_user(app, "pat_fv_b")

    visit_b_id = _create_visit(app, patient_id=pid_b, clinician_id=cid)

    _login(client, "pat_fv_a")
    token = _start_and_fill_assessment(client, visit_id=None)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {
            "request_token": token,
            "visit_id": str(visit_b_id),
        }),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert b"Invalid visit" in resp.data or resp.status_code in (302, 400)


def test_submit_accepts_own_visit_id(client, app):
    """Patient can bind an assessment to their own visit."""
    cid = _create_clinician(app, "doc_ov")
    pid = _create_user(app, "pat_ov")
    vid = _create_visit(app, patient_id=pid, clinician_id=cid)

    _login(client, "pat_ov")
    token = _start_and_fill_assessment(client, visit_id=vid)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {
            "request_token": token,
            "visit_id": str(vid),
        }),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    # Should succeed — redirects to result page
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Invalid visit" not in resp.data


def test_submit_without_visit_id_succeeds(client, app):
    """submit() must succeed when no visit_id is provided."""
    pid = _create_user(app, "pat_novid")
    _login(client, "pat_novid")

    token = _start_and_fill_assessment(client, visit_id=None)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Invalid visit" not in resp.data
"}

42	    "bp_category": "Crisis",

44	    "med_adherence": "often_miss",
45	}

48	# ── Scoring engine unit tests ──
49	
50	def test_scoring_low_risk():
51	    scores = calculate_scores(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
52	    risk, explanations = calculate_risk_level(scores)
53	    assert risk == "Low"
54	    assert scores["phq9"]["total"] == 0
55	    assert scores["gad7"]["total"] == 0
56	
57	
58	def test_scoring_high_risk():

61	    assert risk == "High"
62	    assert scores["phq9"]["total"] == 27
63	    assert scores["gad7"]["total"] == 21
64	    assert len(explanations) >= 3
65	
66	
67	def test_scoring_moderate_phq9():
68	    answers = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
69	    # PHQ-9 total = 12 (moderate)
70	    for i in range(1, 5):
71	        answers[f"phq9_q{i}"] = "3"
72	    scores = calculate_scores(answers)
73	    risk, _ = calculate_risk_level(scores)
74	    assert risk == "Moderate"

82	    assert risk == "Moderate"

88	    answers["dizziness"] = "yes"
89	    scores = calculate_scores(answers)
90	    risk, _ = calculate_risk_level(scores)
91	    assert risk == "High"

94	def test_phq9_severity_labels():
95	    for total, expected in [(0, "Minimal"), (5, "Mild"), (10, "Moderate"), (15, "Moderately Severe"), (20, "Severe")]:
96	        answers = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
97	        remaining = total
98	        for i in range(1, 10):
99	            val = min(3, remaining)
100	            answers[f"phq9_q{i}"] = str(val)
101	            remaining -= val

103	        assert scores["phq9"]["severity"] == expected, f"Expected {expected} for total {total}"

132

153	def test_assessment_idempotency(client, app):
154	    _create_user(app, "pat_a4")
155	    _login(client, "pat_a4")

157

165	    # Submit again with same token (different nonce — idempotency key is request_token, not nonce)
166	    resp = client.post("/assessments/submit", data=signed_data("POST", _SUBMIT_PATH, {"request_token": "tok-idem"}), follow_redirects=True)
167	    assert resp.status_code == 200

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# visit_id validation tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _create_clinician_for_assessment(app, username):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
        user.set_password("Password1")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        c = Clinician(user_id=user.id)
        db.session.add(c)
        db.session.commit()
        return c.id


def _create_visit_for(app, patient_id, clinician_id):
    with app.app_context():
        visit = Visit(patient_id=patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id, status="booked")
        db.session.add(visit)
        db.session.commit()
        return visit.id


def _fill_draft(client, visit_id=None):
    """Walk through all wizard steps so a draft exists."""
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    token = "tok-visit-val"
    all_data["request_token"] = token
    if visit_id:
        all_data["visit_id"] = str(visit_id)
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/step/{step}", data=all_data)
    return token


def test_submit_rejects_nonexistent_visit_id(client, app):
    """submit() must reject a visit_id that doesn't exist in the DB."""
    _create_user(app, "pat_vv1")
    _login(client, "pat_vv1")
    _fill_draft(client)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": "tok-visit-val", "visit_id": "99999"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert b"Invalid visit" in resp.data


def test_submit_rejects_foreign_visit(client, app):
    """Patient A cannot bind an assessment to Patient B's visit."""
    cid = _create_clinician_for_assessment(app, "doc_fv2")
    pid_a = _create_user(app, "pat_fv2a")
    pid_b = _create_user(app, "pat_fv2b")
    vid_b = _create_visit_for(app, patient_id=pid_b, clinician_id=cid)

    _login(client, "pat_fv2a")
    _fill_draft(client)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": "tok-visit-val", "visit_id": str(vid_b)}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert b"Invalid visit" in resp.data


def test_submit_accepts_own_visit(client, app):
    """Patient binding assessment to their own visit must succeed."""
    cid = _create_clinician_for_assessment(app, "doc_ov2")
    pid = _create_user(app, "pat_ov2")
    vid = _create_visit_for(app, patient_id=pid, clinician_id=cid)

    _login(client, "pat_ov2")
    _fill_draft(client, visit_id=vid)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": "tok-visit-val", "visit_id": str(vid)}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"Invalid visit" not in resp.data"}

def test_change_role_requires_reason(client, app):

def test_change_role_creates_audit_record(client, app):
    """Successful role change creates an AuditLog with before/after/reason."""
    from app.models.audit import AuditLog
    import json

    _create_user(app, "admin_al1", role="administrator")
    uid = _create_user(app, "user_al1", role="patient")
    _login(client, "admin_al1")

    path = f"/admin/users/{uid}/role"
    client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"role": "clinician", "reason": "Credential verified"}),
        headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
    )

    with app.app_context():
        entry = AuditLog.query.filter_by(action="change_role").order_by(AuditLog.id.desc()).first()
        assert entry is not None
        details = json.loads(entry.details_json)
        assert details["before"] == "patient"
        assert details["after"] == "clinician"
        assert details["reason"] == "Credential verified"


def test_change_status_creates_audit_record(client, app):
    """Successful status change creates an AuditLog with before/after/reason."""
    from app.models.audit import AuditLog
    import json

    uid = _create_user(app, "user_al2", role="patient")

        details = json.loads(entry.details_json)
        assert details["before"] is True
        assert details["after"] is False
        assert details["reason"] == "Suspended pending review""}

1	# MeridianCare Clinic Operations Platform
2	
3	A comprehensive clinic operations platform built with Flask, SQLite, and HTMX.
4	
5	## Requirements
6	
7	- Python 3.10+ (local development and unit tests)
8	- Docker (production deployment and E2E tests)
9	
10	## Environment Variables (Production)
11	
12	The following environment variables **must** be set when running with `FLASK_ENV=production`:
13	
14	| Variable | Description |
15	|---|---|
16	| `SECRET_KEY` | Stable secret key for Flask session signing (e.g. `python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"`) |
17	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | Stable Fernet key for field-level encryption (e.g. `python -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())"`) |
18	| `REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET` | Stable HMAC secret used to sign anti-replay request fields (`_nonce`, `_timestamp`, `_signature` / `X-Signature`) (e.g. `python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"`) |
19	
20	The app will raise `RuntimeError` at startup if any of these are missing in production.
21	In development and testing, random/default keys are generated automatically.
22	
23	## Quick Start
24	
25	```bash
26	# Install dependencies
27	pip install -r requirements.txt
28	
29	# Run in development mode (auto-generates ephemeral keys — not for production)
30	FLASK_ENV=development python run.py

content.text:
30	FLASK_ENV=development python run.py
31	```
32	
33	Visit `https://localhost:5000` in your browser. Accept the self-signed certificate warning.
34	
35	> **Note:** `python run.py` defaults to `production` mode. Set `FLASK_ENV=development` for local
36	> development (ephemeral keys are generated automatically). For production, set the required
37	> environment variables listed above before starting the app.
38	
39	## Docker (Production Deployment)
40	
41	Docker is the recommended way to run MeridianCare in production. The `Dockerfile` builds a
42	self-contained image that runs under `FLASK_ENV=production` and generates its own self-signed
43	TLS certificate at build time.
44	
45	### Build and run manually
46	
47	```bash
48	# Build the image
49	docker build -t meridiancare .
50	
51	# Generate secrets (run once, store the output securely)
52	export SECRET_KEY=$(python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))")
53	export ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(python -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())")
54	export REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET=$(python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))")
55	
56	# Run the container
57	docker run -d \
58	  -p 5000:5000 \
59	  -e FLASK_ENV=production \
60	  -e SECRET_KEY="$SECRET_KEY" \
61	  -e ENCRYPTION_KEY="$ENCRYPTION_KEY" \
62	  -e REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET="$REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET" \
63	  --name meridiancare \
64	  meridiancare
65	```
66	
67	Visit `https://localhost:5000`. Accept the self-signed certificate warning (replace with a CA-issued
68	certificate for internet-facing deployments).
69	
70	> **Important:** The three secret environment variables must be kept stable across restarts.
71	> Changing `SECRET_KEY` invalidates all active sessions; changing `ENCRYPTION_KEY` makes
72	> previously encrypted patient data unreadable.
73	
74	### `docker-compose.yml` — E2E testing only
75	
76	The included `docker-compose.yml` contains **hardcoded example keys** intended solely for the
77	automated E2E test suite. **Do not use it for any real deployment.** Always supply your own
78	secrets via environment variables as shown above.
79	
80	## Running Tests
81	
82	### Unit/Integration Tests Only
83	
84	```bash
85	pip install -r requirements-test.txt
86	python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e/ -v
87	```
88	
89	### Full Test Suite (Unit + E2E via Docker)

content.text:
88	
89	### Full Test Suite (Unit + E2E via Docker)
90	
91	```bash
92	bash run_tests.sh
93	```
94	
95	This script will:
96	1. Run unit/integration tests via pytest
97	2. Check that Docker is running
98	3. Build and start the app in a Docker container
99	4. Wait for the `/health` endpoint to respond
100	5. Run Playwright E2E tests against the containerized app
101	6. Tear down the Docker container
102	7. Exit with code 0 on success, non-zero on failure
103	
104	## Project Structure
105	
106	```
107	repo/
108	├── app/
109	│   ├── __init__.py          # App factory
110	│   ├── config.py            # Configuration classes
111	│   ├── extensions.py        # Flask extensions
112	│   ├── models/              # Database models
113	│   ├── routes/              # Blueprint route modules
114	│   ├── templates/           # Jinja2 templates
115	│   ├── static/              # CSS, JS, images
116	│   └── utils/               # Helpers (logging, crypto, etc.)
117	├── migrations/              # DB migration scripts
118	├── certs/                   # Self-signed certificates (auto-generated)
119	├── tests/                   # Unit & integration tests

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\README.md", "old_string": "## Project Structure", "new_string": "## Security & Audit Features

### Encrypted Clinical Notes
Clinical notes are stored encrypted at rest using Fernet symmetric encryption (same key as all other field-level encryption). The `ClinicalNote` model exposes a `content` property that decrypts on access — the raw `content_encrypted` column is never exposed in responses or logs. Access control: patients may read their own notes via `GET /notes/my`; clinicians, front-desk staff, and administrators create and read notes via `GET/POST /notes/patient/<id>`.

### Admin Mutations — Reason Capture & Audit Trail
Both `change_role` and `change_status` in the admin panel require a non-empty `reason` field. On success, an `AuditLog` entry is written including: actor user ID, action (`change_role` / `change_status`), target user ID, before/after values, and the provided reason. Requests without a reason are rejected with HTTP 400.

### Assessment visit_id Validation
When a patient submits a health assessment with a `visit_id`, the route verifies the visit exists in the database and — for patient-role users — that `visit.patient_id` matches the submitting user. Invalid or unauthorized `visit_id` values return a validation error without creating an `AssessmentResult`.

## Project Structure"}
