# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Token-at-rest protection tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_assessment_request_token_stored_hashed(client, app):
    """AssessmentResult.request_token must be SHA-256 hash of the raw token."""
    import hashlib
    _create_user(app, "pat_tok_hash")
    _login(client, "pat_tok_hash")
    client.get("/assessments/start")

    raw_token = "plaintext-token-should-not-appear-in-db"
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = raw_token
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/step/{step}", data=all_data)

    client.post(
        _SUBMIT_PATH,
        data=signed_data("POST", _SUBMIT_PATH, {"request_token": raw_token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )

    with app.app_context():
        result = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(
            patient_id=User.query.filter_by(username="pat_tok_hash").first().id
        ).first()
        assert result is not None
        # Raw value must NOT be stored.
        assert result.request_token != raw_token
        # Must match the SHA-256 hex digest.
        expected = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
        assert result.request_token == expected


def test_assessment_idempotency_still_works_with_hashed_token(client, app):
    """Duplicate submissions with the same token return the same result after hashing."""
    import hashlib
    _create_user(app, "pat_tok_idem2")
    _login(client, "pat_tok_idem2")
    client.get("/assessments/start")

    raw_token = "idempotency-test-token-xyz"
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = raw_token
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/step/{step}", data=all_data)

    # First submit — creates the result.
    resp1 = client.post(
        _SUBMIT_PATH,
        data=signed_data("POST", _SUBMIT_PATH, {"request_token": raw_token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp1.status_code == 200

    # Second submit — must be idempotent (redirects to same result, no new row).
    resp2 = client.post(
        _SUBMIT_PATH,
        data=signed_data("POST", _SUBMIT_PATH, {"request_token": raw_token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
        count = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(request_token=token_hash).count()
        assert count == 1  # exactly one result despite two submits"}

        assert result.request_token != raw_token
        # Must match the SHA-256 hex digest.
        expected = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
        assert result.request_token == expected


def test_assessment_idempotency_still_works_with_hashed_token(client, app):
    """Duplicate submissions with the same token return the same result after hashing."""
    import hashlib
    _create_user(app, "pat_tok_idem2")
    _login(client, "pat_tok_idem2")
    client.get("/assessments/start")

    raw_token = "idempotency-test-token-xyz"
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = raw_token
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/step/{step}", data=all_data)

    # First submit — creates the result.
    resp1 = client.post(
        _SUBMIT_PATH,
        data=signed_data("POST", _SUBMIT_PATH, {"request_token": raw_token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp1.status_code == 200

    # Second submit — must be idempotent (redirects to same result, no new row).
    resp2 = client.post(
        _SUBMIT_PATH,
        data=signed_data("POST", _SUBMIT_PATH, {"request_token": raw_token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        token_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_token.encode()).hexdigest()
        count = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(request_token=token_hash).count()
        assert count == 1  # exactly one result despite two submits"}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\tests\test_user_isolation.py", "content": """"User-switch isolation tests.

Verify that after logout/login as a different user, no stale user-specific
content from the previous session is accessible or visible in key views.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, time, timedelta, datetime, timezone
from app.models.user import User
from app.models.assessment import AssessmentResult
from app.models.scheduling import Clinician, Slot, Reservation
from app.models.visit import Visit
from app.extensions import db
from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role=role)
        user.set_password(password)
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        return user.id


def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


def _logout(client):
    return client.post("/auth/logout", follow_redirects=True)


def _create_clinician_with_slot(app, username, slot_date=None):
    """Return (patient_user_id, clinician_id, slot_id)."""
    from datetime import date as _date, time as _time
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
        user.set_password("Password1")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        c = Clinician(user_id=user.id)
        db.session.add(c)
        db.session.commit()
        slot = Slot(
            clinician_id=c.id,
            date=slot_date or (_date.today() + timedelta(days=3)),
            start_time=_time(10, 0),
            end_time=_time(10, 30),
            capacity=5,
            status="available",
        )
        db.session.add(slot)
        db.session.commit()
        return c.id, slot.id


LOW_RISK_ANSWERS = {
    **{f"phq9_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 10)},
    **{f"gad7_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 8)},
    "bp_category": "Normal",
    "fall_history": "no", "mobility_aids": "no", "dizziness": "no", "balance_meds": "no",
    "med_adherence": "never_miss",
}

_SUBMIT_PATH = "/assessments/submit"


def _submit_assessment(client, token):
    client.get("/assessments/start")
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = token
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/step/{step}", data=all_data)
    client.post(
        _SUBMIT_PATH,
        data=signed_data("POST", _SUBMIT_PATH, {"request_token": token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assessment history isolation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_user_switch_assessment_history_isolated(client, app):
    """User B's assessment history page must not show User A's results."""
    pid_a = _create_user(app, "iso_assess_a")
    pid_b = _create_user(app, "iso_assess_b")

    # User A submits an assessment.
    _login(client, "iso_assess_a")
    _submit_assessment(client, "iso-tok-a")
    _logout(client)

    # User B logs in — their history must be empty.
    _login(client, "iso_assess_b")
    resp = client.get("/assessments/history")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    # User A's result must not appear in User B's history page.
    with app.app_context():
        a_result = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid_a).first()
        assert a_result is not None  # sanity: A's result exists
        # B's history page must not link to A's result.
        assert f"/assessments/result/{a_result.id}".encode() not in resp.data


def test_user_a_result_access_denied_to_user_b(client, app):
    """User B must receive a redirect/403 when directly accessing User A's result."""
    pid_a = _create_user(app, "iso_result_a")
    pid_b = _create_user(app, "iso_result_b")

    _login(client, "iso_result_a")
    _submit_assessment(client, "iso-tok-res-a")
    _logout(client)

    with app.app_context():
        a_result = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid_a).first()
        result_id = a_result.id

    # User B tries to access User A's result directly.
    _login(client, "iso_result_b")
    resp = client.get(f"/assessments/result/{result_id}", follow_redirects=True)
    # Must not display A's data — either denied or redirected away.
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 403)
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        # If it redirected to history (200 after redirect), A's result content
        # must not be rendered — check that the result ID is not present.
        assert b"Low" not in resp.data or b"Assessment History" in resp.data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Appointment / reservation isolation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_user_switch_reservation_isolation(client, app):
    """User B's appointments page must not show User A's reservations."""
    cid, sid = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "doc_iso_res")
    pid_a = _create_user(app, "iso_res_a")
    pid_b = _create_user(app, "iso_res_b")

    # User A holds a slot.
    _login(client, "iso_res_a")
    path = f"/schedule/hold/{sid}"
    client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path), follow_redirects=True)
    _logout(client)

    # Confirm User A has a reservation.
    with app.app_context():
        a_res = Reservation.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid_a).first()
        assert a_res is not None

    # User B logs in and views their appointments.
    _login(client, "iso_res_b")
    resp = client.get("/schedule/my-appointments")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    # User A's reservation ID must not appear in User B's page.
    with app.app_context():
        a_res = Reservation.query.filter_by(patient_id=pid_a).first()
        assert f"/schedule/confirm/{a_res.id}".encode() not in resp.data
        assert f"/schedule/cancel/{a_res.id}".encode() not in resp.data


def test_user_b_cannot_access_user_a_confirm_page(client, app):
    """User B must not be able to access User A's reservation confirm page."""
    cid, sid = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "doc_iso_confirm")
    pid_a = _create_user(app, "iso_confirm_a")
    pid_b = _create_user(app, "iso_confirm_b")

    # Create reservation for User A directly.
    with app.app_context():
        res = Reservation(
            slot_id=sid,
            patient_id=pid_a,
            status="held",
            held_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(minutes=10),
        )
        db.session.add(res)
        db.session.commit()
        res_id = res.id

    # User B tries to access the confirm page for A's reservation.
    _login(client, "iso_confirm_b")
    resp = client.get(f"/schedule/confirm/{res_id}", follow_redirects=True)
    # Must not show A's reservation — either 403 or redirect back to available.
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 403)
    if resp.status_code == 200:
        # If redirected to available page, A's slot details must not be shown
        # under B's identity (the "Reservation not found" flash fires instead).
        assert b"Reservation not found" in resp.data or b"Available" in resp.data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Session does not persist previous user's identity
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_logout_clears_session_user_identity(client, app):
    """After logout and re-login as different user, nav reflects new user only."""
    _create_user(app, "iso_nav_a")
    _create_user(app, "iso_nav_b")

    _login(client, "iso_nav_a")
    resp_a = client.get("/")
    assert b"iso_nav_a" in resp_a.data
    assert b"iso_nav_b" not in resp_a.data

    _logout(client)

    _login(client, "iso_nav_b")
    resp_b = client.get("/")
    assert b"iso_nav_b" in resp_b.data
    # User A's username must not appear after switching.
    assert b"iso_nav_a" not in resp_b.data


def test_unauthenticated_after_logout_redirected(client, app):
    """After logout, accessing a protected page redirects to login."""
    _create_user(app, "iso_redirect_a")
    _login(client, "iso_redirect_a")
    _logout(client)

    resp = client.get("/assessments/history", follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code == 302
    assert "login" in resp.headers.get("Location", "").lower()
"}

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\README.md", "old_string": "## Account Deletion & Anonymization", "new_string": "## Token-at-Rest Protection

All request/idempotency tokens are stored as **SHA-256 hex digests** — the raw client-supplied value is never written to the database or application logs.

| Column | Model | Storage |
|---|---|---|
| `AssessmentResult.request_token` | `app/models/assessment.py` | SHA-256 hash of the submitted token |
| `Reservation.request_token` | `app/models/scheduling.py` | SHA-256 hash of the submitted token |
| `RequestToken.token` | `app/models/idempotency.py` | SHA-256 hash (via `app/utils/idempotency.py`) |
| Anti-replay nonces (`SignedRequest.nonce`) | `app/models/audit.py` | SHA-256 hash (via `app/utils/antireplay.py`) |

Idempotency lookups hash the incoming token before querying, so duplicate-detection still works correctly without exposing the raw value.

## Time-Driven Hold Expiry

Reservation holds (10-minute window) are expired by **two complementary mechanisms**:

1. **Scheduled job** (`hold_expiry`, every 5 min) — runs inside the APScheduler background thread started by `create_app()`. Holds are expired on a fixed cadence regardless of user traffic. This is the authoritative mechanism.
2. **Lazy cleanup** (`schedule_bp.before_request`) — `expire_stale_holds()` is also called on every request to the schedule blueprint. This provides immediate cleanup for active users but is not relied upon alone.

The scheduler is skipped in `testing` mode. Tests that need to verify time-driven expiry call `expire_stale_holds()` directly to simulate a scheduler firing.

```python
# Verify fixes without Docker:
python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e/ -v
# Targeted token-at-rest tests:
python -m pytest tests/test_assessments.py tests/test_scheduling.py -k "hash" -v
# Targeted expiry tests:
python -m pytest tests/test_scheduling.py -k "expiry" -v
# User-switch isolation:
python -m pytest tests/test_user_isolation.py -v
```

## Account Deletion & Anonymization"}

| `AssessmentResult.request_token` | `app/models/assessment.py` | SHA-256 hash of the submitted token |
| `Reservation.request_token` | `app/models/scheduling.py` | SHA-256 hash of the submitted token |
| `RequestToken.token` | `app/models/idempotency.py` | SHA-256 hash (via `app/utils/idempotency.py`) |
| Anti-replay nonces (`SignedRequest.nonce`) | `app/models/audit.py` | SHA-256 hash (via `app/utils/antireplay.py`) |

Idempotency lookups hash the incoming token before querying, so duplicate-detection still works correctly without exposing the raw value.

## Time-Driven Hold Expiry

Reservation holds (10-minute window) are expired by **two complementary mechanisms**:

1. **Scheduled job** (`hold_expiry`, every 5 min) — runs inside the APScheduler background thread started by `create_app()`. Holds are expired on a fixed cadence regardless of user traffic. This is the authoritative mechanism.
2. **Lazy cleanup** (`schedule_bp.before_request`) — `expire_stale_holds()` is also called on every request to the schedule blueprint. This provides immediate cleanup for active users but is not relied upon alone.

The scheduler is skipped in `testing` mode. Tests that need to verify time-driven expiry call `expire_stale_holds()` directly to simulate a scheduler firing.

```python
# Verify fixes without Docker:
python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e/ -v
# Targeted token-at-rest tests:
python -m pytest tests/test_assessments.py tests/test_scheduling.py -k "hash" -v
# Targeted expiry tests:
python -m pytest tests/test_scheduling.py -k "expiry" -v
# User-switch isolation:
python -m pytest tests/test_user_isolation.py -v
```

## Account Deletion & Anonymization"}

## Summary

### Files changed and why

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `app/utils/idempotency.py` | Exposed `hash_token` as a public alias for `_hash_token` so routes can import a shared SHA-256 utility rather than duplicating it |
| `app/routes/assessments.py` | Import `hash_token`; hash token before idempotency lookup and before write to `AssessmentResult.request_token` |
| `app/routes/schedule.py` | Import `hash_token`; hash raw token before writing to `Reservation.request_token` in `hold()` |
| `app/__init__.py` | Renamed `_start_reminder_scheduler` → `_start_background_scheduler`; added `hold_expiry` APScheduler job (every 5 min) alongside the existing `reminder_generation` job (every 15 min) |
| `tests/test_assessments.py` | Updated `test_assessment_idempotency` to query by hash instead of raw token; added `test_assessment_request_token_stored_hashed` and `test_assessment_idempotency_still_works_with_hashed_token` |
| `tests/test_scheduling.py` | Added `test_reservation_request_token_stored_hashed`, `test_reservation_token_none_when_not_provided`, `test_hold_expiry_time_driven_without_route`, `test_hold_expiry_leaves_active_holds` |
| `tests/test_user_isolation.py` | New file — 7 tests covering user-switch isolation across assessment history, result access, reservation visibility, confirm-page access, nav identity, and post-logout redirect |
| `README.md` | Added "Token-at-Rest Protection" and "Time-Driven Hold Expiry" sections with table and verification commands |

### Test results

```
298 passed, 0 failed, 3 warnings  (119 s)
```
(286 before → +12 new tests)

### Remaining risks / migration caveats

- **Existing rows with raw tokens**: Any `AssessmentResult` or `Reservation` rows already in a production SQLite DB will have raw tokens. The idempotency check will miss them (hash ≠ raw), so re-submitting an old token would create a duplicate result rather than deduplicating. Mitigation: run a one-off migration script `UPDATE … SET request_token = sha256(request_token)` against live data before deploying. For assessment results this is low-risk since tokens are UUID-based and short-lived. A comment in the code marks this.
- **Scheduler not tested live**: The APScheduler itself is skipped in test mode. The tests prove that `expire_stale_holds()` works correctly when called directly; the scheduler wiring is verified by reading the `app/__init__.py` setup, not via an integration test against a live timer.
- **Column width**: SHA-256 hex is exactly 64 chars — fits `String(64)` without schema change.

One small caveat still worth noting:
- Hold expiry job runs every 5 minutes (app/__init__.py:58), so a hold can remain blocked slightly beyond 10 minutes until next sweep; functionality is now time-driven, but not exact-to-the-minute strict.

Good catch. Tightening the sweep interval from 5 minutes to 1 minute reduces the maximum overage from ~15 minutes to ~11 minutes — close enough to exact for practical purposes — and the fix is one line.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The session is a continuation of a long multi-session implementation effort for MeridianCare, a Flask+SQLite+HTMX clinic operations platform. The sequence of user requests was:

**Session 1 (from prior context):** Implement three audit remediation items: (1) encrypted clinical notes with RBAC, (2) admin mutations (change_role/change_status) requiring a `reason` field and audit logging, (3) assessment `visit_id` ownership validation.

**Session 2:** Fix account deletion anonymization gap — `POST /patient/delete-account` was leaving clinical records (notes, drafts, change logs, login attempts) with PII linkage intact.

**Session 3:** Fix two audit findings: (1) `/coverage/check` only matched by ZIP, needed distance band + neighborhood grouping; (2) `/patient/demographics/reveal` lacked anti-replay protection.

**Session 4:** Address three audit gaps toward moving from Partial Pass to Pass: (1) `AssessmentResult.request_token` and `Reservation.request_token` stored as raw strings; (2) reservation hold expiry was lazy/request-triggered rather than time-driven; (3) missing targeted tests for the above plus user-switch isolation.

**Session 5:** User noted hold expiry at 5-min sweep means holds can last ~15 min past their 10-min window. Fix: tighten sweep to 1 minute (max ~11 min overage).

**Session 6 (most recent):** User noted two stale "every 5 minutes" doc strings remain in `app/__init__.py` at lines 15 and 49, now inconsistent with the 1-minute actual schedule.

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- Flask Blueprint pattern, SQLAlchemy, SQLite in-memory test DB via `db.create_all()`
- Fernet symmetric encryption (`encrypt_value`/`decrypt_value`) for clinical note content
- HMAC-SHA256 anti-replay via `@antireplay` decorator; `signed_data()` test helper
- `log_action()` audit trail utility with before/after/reason in `details_json`
- SHA-256 hashing of idempotency/request tokens before DB storage (`hash_token` from `app/utils/idempotency.py`)
- APScheduler `BackgroundScheduler` for time-driven periodic jobs (hold expiry + reminder generation)
- HTMX `hx-headers` with server-generated `antireplay_headers()` Jinja2 context processor
- `db.session.no_autoflush` context manager to prevent premature flush with pending NOT NULL changes
- Flask-Login `load_user` returning `None` for inactive users
- `expire_stale_holds()` in `app/models/scheduling.py` as the shared expiry function

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`app/models/clinical_note.py`** — ClinicalNote model with `content_encrypted` (Fernet), `content` property decrypts at render time, `create()` classmethod encrypts.

- **`app/models/__init__.py`** — Added `from app.models.clinical_note import ClinicalNote`.

- **`app/models/coverage.py`** — Added `neighborhoods_json = db.Column(db.JSON, nullable=True, default=list)` to `CoverageZone` for neighborhood-based zone matching.

- **`app/routes/notes.py`** (new) — Blueprint at `/notes`; `GET/POST /notes/patient/<id>` staff-only with `@antireplay`, `GET /notes/my` patient read-own.

- **`app/routes/admin.py`** — Added `import json` and `from app.utils.audit import log_action`; both `change_role` and `change_status` now require non-empty `reason` field (400 if missing) and call `log_action()` with `before`/`after`/`reason` in `details_json`.

- **`app/routes/assessments.py`** — Added `from app.models.visit import Visit` for visit_id validation in `submit()`; added `from app.utils.idempotency import hash_token as _hash_token`; idempotency check now uses `_hash_token(request_token)` before query; stored value is `_hash_token(request_token) if request_token else None`.
  ```python
  if request_token:
      token_hash = _hash_token(request_token)
      existing = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(request_token=token_hash).first()
  ...
  request_token=_hash_token(request_token) if request_token else None,
  ```

- **`tests/test_demographics.py`** — Updated `test_reveal_field` to use `signed_data`.

- **`README.md`** — Added sections: Account Deletion & Anonymization, Token-at-Rest Protection, Time-Driven Hold Expiry (updated to say "every 1 min" and "~11 minutes" bound).

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **`test_reveal_field` broke** after `@antireplay` added to `reveal_field()`: existing test sent POST without signed fields → 400. Fixed by adding `signed_data("POST", _reveal_path, {"field": "insurance_id"})`.

Additionally, `DemographicsChangeLog.query.update()` triggered autoflush of dirty demo object before commit. Fixed by using placeholder values (`"0000000"` / `date(1900,1,1)`) and wrapping the bulk update in `with db.session.no_autoflush:`.

Perfect! I've successfully read all 10 files you requested. Here's a comprehensive summary of the complete file contents:

## File Reading Summary

I have successfully retrieved the full contents of all requested files from the Flask clinic app:

### 1. **app/routes/assessments.py** (250 lines)
Main assessment workflow routes handling:
- `/start` - Initiates assessment wizard with draft recovery
- `/step/<step>` - Processes wizard steps and saves drafts
- `/submit` - Submits completed assessment with idempotency check and anti-replay protection
- `/result/<assessment_id>` - Views assessment results
- `/history` - Patient views own assessment history
- `/save-draft` - Auto-saves draft answers
- `/patient/<patient_id>` - Staff views patient assessments

Key features: Uses `@antireplay` decorator, implements idempotent token hashing, manages assessment drafts.

### 2. **app/routes/schedule.py** (389 lines)
Scheduling system routes including:
- `/available` - Shows available slots with date/clinician filtering
- `/hold/<slot_id>` - Creates 10-minute hold on slot (max 2 simultaneous)
- `/confirm/<reservation_id>` - Confirms/completes booking with Visit creation
- `/cancel/<reservation_id>` - Cancels reservations
- `/my-appointments` - Patient views own appointments
- `/staff/calendar` - Staff weekly calendar view
- `/admin/holidays` - Holiday management (blocks slots)
- `/admin/bulk-generate` - Bulk generate slots from templates

Features: Anti-replay protection, expiry of stale holds, room conflict checking, idempotent Visit creation.

### 3. **app/routes/notes.py** (64 lines)
Clinical notes management:
- `/patient/<patient_id>` - Staff creates and views patient notes
- `/my` - Patient views own notes only

Uses `@antireplay` decorator and staff role restriction.

### 4. **app/models/assessment.py** (72 lines)
Three SQLAlchemy models:
- **AssessmentTemplate** - Question/scoring templates with versioning
- **AssessmentResult** - Stores completed assessments with risk levels and hashed request tokens
- **AssessmentDraft** - Partial assessment progress with answer snapshots

Uses JSON columns for questions, answers, scores, and explanations.

### 5. **app/models/scheduling.py** (117 lines)
Six core scheduling models:
- **Clinician** - Links users to clinician profiles with specialty/slot duration
- **ScheduleTemplate** - Recurring schedule patterns (day/time/duration/capacity)
- **Room** - Physical rooms for appointments
- **Slot** - Individual appointment slots with availability checking
- **Reservation** - Hold/confirmed bookings with expiry tracking
- **Holiday** - Blocks slots on specified dates
- Helper: `expire_stale_holds()` function

### 6. **app/models/user.py** (60 lines)
Two models:
- **User** - Core user with role-based access (patient/clinician/front_desk/administrator), bcrypt password hashing, is_active flag
- **LoginAttempt** - Tracks login attempts for anomaly detection (IP, user-agent, timestamp)

Includes `load_user` handler for Flask-Login.

### 7. **app/utils/audit.py** (138 lines)
Audit and anomaly detection functions:
- `log_action()` - Records audit events with user/IP/user-agent context
- `check_new_device_alert()` - Creates AnomalyAlert for new IP/device logins with 24-hour cooldown deduplication
- `anomaly_detection()` - Detects >5 failed logins in 10 minutes and creates alerts

Uses AuditLog and AnomalyAlert models for persistence.

### 8. **app/utils/antireplay.py** (79 lines)
Comprehensive anti-replay protection decorator:
- `_hash_nonce()` - SHA-256 hashing for safe storage
- `_compute_signature()` - HMAC-SHA256 signature over method|path|nonce|timestamp
- `@antireplay` - Validates signature, checks 5-minute window, deduplicates nonces

Protects against replay attacks with signature verification before nonce storage.

### 9. **tests/conftest.py** (22 lines)
PyTest fixtures:
- `app` - Creates testing app instance
- `client` - Test client for making requests
- `db` - Database with automatic create/drop for test isolation

### 10. **tests/signing_helpers.py** (36 lines)
Test utilities for anti-replay:
- `TEST_SIGNING_SECRET` - Matches testing config for HMAC verification
- `signed_data(method, path, extra)` - Generates form data with valid _nonce, _timestamp, _signature for testing protected endpoints

All files are now available for your implementation work. The codebase shows a well-structured Flask clinic app with strong security patterns including anti-replay protection, audit logging, anomaly detection, and role-based access control.

1	from flask import Blueprint, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, flash
2	from flask_login import login_required, current_user
3	from app.extensions import db
4	from app.models.clinical_note import ClinicalNote
5	from app.models.user import User
6	from app.utils.auth import role_required
7	from app.utils.antireplay import antireplay
8	
9	notes_bp = Blueprint("notes", __name__, url_prefix="/notes")
10	
11	_STAFF_ROLES = ("administrator", "clinician", "front_desk")
12	
13	
14	@notes_bp.route("/patient/<int:patient_id>", methods=["GET", "POST"])
15	@role_required(*_STAFF_ROLES)
16	@antireplay
17	def patient_notes(patient_id):
18	    """Staff view: list all notes for a patient and create new ones."""
19	    patient = db.session.get(User, patient_id)
20	    if not patient:
21	        flash("Patient not found.", "danger")
22	        return redirect(url_for("staff.patient_list"))
23	
24	    if request.method == "POST":
25	        content = request.form.get("content", "").strip()
26	        if not content:
27	            flash("Note content cannot be empty.", "danger")
28	        else:
29	            note = ClinicalNote.create(
30	                patient_id=patient_id,
31	                author_id=current_user.id,
32	                content=content,
33	            )
34	            db.session.add(note)
35	            db.session.commit()
36	            flash("Note saved.", "success")
37	        return redirect(url_for("notes.patient_notes", patient_id=patient_id))
38	
39	    notes = (
40	        ClinicalNote.query
41	        .filter_by(patient_id=patient_id)
42	        .order_by(ClinicalNote.created_at.desc())
43	        .all()
44	    )
45	    return render_template("notes/patient_notes.html", patient=patient, notes=notes)
46	
47	
48	@notes_bp.route("/my")
49	@login_required
50	def my_notes():
51	    """Patient view: read own notes only."""
52	    if current_user.role not in ("patient",):
53	        # Staff should use the patient_notes route
54	        flash("Please use the staff notes view.", "info")
55	        return redirect(url_for("staff.patient_list"))
56	
57	    notes = (
58	        ClinicalNote.query
59	        .filter_by(patient_id=current_user.id)
60	        .order_by(ClinicalNote.created_at.desc())
61	        .all()
62	    )
63	    return render_template("notes/my_notes.html", notes=notes)
64

1	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta, date, time
2	from flask import Blueprint, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, flash, jsonify
3	from flask_login import login_required, current_user
4	from app.extensions import db
5	from app.models.scheduling import (
6	    Clinician, ScheduleTemplate, Room, Slot, Reservation, Holiday, expire_stale_holds,
7	)
8	from app.models.visit import Visit
9	from app.utils.auth import role_required
10	from app.utils.antireplay import antireplay
11	from app.utils.idempotency import hash_token as _hash_token
12	
13	schedule_bp = Blueprint("schedule", __name__, url_prefix="/schedule")
14	
15	HOLD_DURATION = timedelta(minutes=10)
16	MAX_SIMULTANEOUS_HOLDS = 2
17	
18	
19	def _has_room_conflict(room_id, slot_date, start_time, end_time, exclude_slot_id=None):
20	    """Return True if a slot already occupies room_id during [start_time, end_time) on slot_date."""
21	    if room_id is None:
22	        return False
23	    query = Slot.query.filter(
24	        Slot.room_id == room_id,
25	        Slot.date == slot_date,
26	        Slot.start_time < end_time,
27	        Slot.end_time > start_time,
28	    )
29	    if exclude_slot_id is not None:
30	        query = query.filter(Slot.id != exclude_slot_id)
31	    return query.first() is not None
32	
33	
34	@schedule_bp.before_request
35	def cleanup_expired():
36	    """Lazy expiry of stale holds on every schedule request."""
37	    try:
38	        expire_stale_holds()
39	    except Exception:
40	        pass
41	
42	
43	@schedule_bp.route("/available")
44	@login_required
45	def available():
46	    date_from = request.args.get("date_from", date.today().isoformat())
47	    date_to = request.args.get("date_to", (date.today() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat())
48	    clinician_id = request.args.get("clinician_id", type=int)
49	
50	    try:
51	        d_from = datetime.strptime(date_from, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
52	        d_to = datetime.strptime(date_to, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
53	    except ValueError:
54	        d_from = date.today()
55	        d_to = date.today() + timedelta(days=7)
56	
57	    query = Slot.query.filter(
58	        Slot.date >= d_from,
59	        Slot.date <= d_to,
60	        Slot.status == "available",
61	    )
62	    if clinician_id:
63	        query = query.filter(Slot.clinician_id == clinician_id)
64	
65	    slots = query.order_by(Slot.date, Slot.start_time).all()
66	
67	    # Filter to only actually available slots
68	    available_slots = [s for s in slots if s.is_available]
69	
70	    clinicians = Clinician.query.all()
71	    return render_template(
72	        "schedule/available.html",
73	        slots=available_slots,
74	        clinicians=clinicians,
75	        date_from=date_from,
76	        date_to=date_to,
77	        selected_clinician=clinician_id,
78	    )
79	
80	
81	@schedule_bp.route("/hold/<int:slot_id>", methods=["POST"])
82	@login_required
83	@antireplay
84	def hold(slot_id):
85	    slot = db.session.get(Slot, slot_id)
86	    if not slot:
87	        flash("Slot not found.", "danger")
88	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
89	
90	    if slot.date < date.today():
91	        flash("Cannot book a slot in the past.", "danger")
92	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
93	
94	    if not slot.is_available:
95	        flash("This slot is no longer available.", "warning")
96	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
97	
98	    # Check max holds
99	    active_holds = Reservation.query.filter_by(
100	        patient_id=current_user.id, status="held"
101	    ).count()
102	    if active_holds >= MAX_SIMULTANEOUS_HOLDS:
103	        flash(f"You can only hold up to {MAX_SIMULTANEOUS_HOLDS} slots at a time.", "warning")
104	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
105	
106	    raw_token = request.form.get("request_token")
107	    reservation = Reservation(
108	        slot_id=slot.id,
109	        patient_id=current_user.id,
110	        status="held",
111	        held_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
112	        expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) + HOLD_DURATION,
113	        # Store SHA-256 hash of token — raw value never persisted.
114	        request_token=_hash_token(raw_token) if raw_token else None,
115	    )
116	    db.session.add(reservation)
117	    db.session.commit()
118	
119	    return redirect(url_for("schedule.confirm_page", reservation_id=reservation.id))
120	
121	
122	@schedule_bp.route("/confirm/<int:reservation_id>", methods=["GET"])
123	@login_required
124	def confirm_page(reservation_id):
125	    reservation = db.session.get(Reservation, reservation_id)
126	    if not reservation or reservation.patient_id != current_user.id:
127	        flash("Reservation not found.", "danger")
128	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
129	
130	    if reservation.is_expired():
131	        reservation.status = "expired"
132	        db.session.commit()
133	        flash("Your reservation hold has expired.", "warning")
134	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
135	
136	    remaining = 0
137	    if reservation.expires_at:
138	        exp = reservation.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if reservation.expires_at.tzinfo is None else reservation.expires_at
139	        remaining = max(0, int((exp - datetime.now(timezone.utc)).total_seconds()))
140	
141	    return render_template(
142	        "schedule/confirm.html",
143	        reservation=reservation,
144	        slot=reservation.slot,
145	        remaining_seconds=remaining,
146	    )
147	
148	
149	@schedule_bp.route("/confirm/<int:reservation_id>", methods=["POST"])
150	@login_required
151	@antireplay
152	def confirm(reservation_id):
153	    reservation = db.session.get(Reservation, reservation_id)
154	    if not reservation or reservation.patient_id != current_user.id:
155	        flash("Reservation not found.", "danger")
156	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
157	
158	    if reservation.is_expired():
159	        reservation.status = "expired"
160	        db.session.commit()
161	        flash("Your reservation hold has expired.", "warning")
162	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.available"))
163	
164	    if reservation.status == "confirmed":
165	        flash("This reservation is already confirmed.", "info")
166	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.my_appointments"))
167	
168	    reservation.status = "confirmed"
169	    reservation.confirmed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
170	    reservation.slot.booked_count += 1
171	
172	    # Idempotently create a Visit record for this booking.
173	    # Using slot_id + patient_id as the uniqueness key prevents duplicates
174	    # even if confirm is somehow called twice on separate requests.
175	    existing_visit = Visit.query.filter_by(
176	        patient_id=reservation.patient_id,
177	        slot_id=reservation.slot_id,
178	    ).first()
179	    if not existing_visit:
180	        visit = Visit(
181	            patient_id=reservation.patient_id,
182	            clinician_id=reservation.slot.clinician_id,
183	            slot_id=reservation.slot_id,
184	            status="booked",
185	        )
186	        db.session.add(visit)
187	
188	    db.session.commit()
189	
190	    flash("Appointment confirmed!", "success")
191	    return redirect(url_for("schedule.my_appointments"))
192	
193	
194	@schedule_bp.route("/cancel/<int:reservation_id>", methods=["POST"])
195	@login_required
196	@antireplay
197	def cancel(reservation_id):
198	    reservation = db.session.get(Reservation, reservation_id)
199	    if not reservation:
200	        flash("Reservation not found.", "danger")
201	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.my_appointments"))
202	
203	    # Allow patient to cancel own, or staff to cancel any
204	    if reservation.patient_id != current_user.id and current_user.role not in ("administrator", "front_desk"):
205	        flash("Access denied.", "danger")
206	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.my_appointments"))
207	
208	    if reservation.status == "confirmed":
209	        reservation.slot.booked_count = max(0, reservation.slot.booked_count - 1)
210	    reservation.status = "canceled"
211	    db.session.commit()
212	
213	    flash("Reservation canceled.", "info")
214	    return redirect(url_for("schedule.my_appointments"))
215	
216	
217	@schedule_bp.route("/my-appointments")
218	@login_required
219	def my_appointments():
220	    reservations = Reservation.query.filter_by(
221	        patient_id=current_user.id
222	    ).filter(
223	        Reservation.status.in_(["held", "confirmed"])
224	    ).order_by(Reservation.held_at.desc()).all()
225	    return render_template("schedule/my_appointments.html", reservations=reservations)
226

228	# ── Staff calendar view ──
229	@schedule_bp.route("/staff/calendar")
230	@role_required("administrator", "clinician", "front_desk")
231	def staff_calendar():
232	    week_str = request.args.get("week")
233	    clinician_id = request.args.get("clinician_id", type=int)
234	
235	    if week_str:
236	        try:
237	            week_start = datetime.strptime(week_str, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
238	        except ValueError:
239	            week_start = date.today() - timedelta(days=date.today().weekday())
240	    else:
241	        week_start = date.today() - timedelta(days=date.today().weekday())
242	
243	    week_end = week_start + timedelta(days=6)
244	
245	    query = Slot.query.filter(Slot.date >= week_start, Slot.date <= week_end)
246	    if clinician_id:
247	        query = query.filter(Slot.clinician_id == clinician_id)
248	    slots = query.order_by(Slot.date, Slot.start_time).all()
249	
250	    clinicians = Clinician.query.all()
251	    holidays = Holiday.query.filter(Holiday.date >= week_start, Holiday.date <= week_end).all()
252	    holiday_dates = {h.date for h in holidays}
253	
254	    prev_week = (week_start - timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
255	    next_week = (week_start + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
256	
257	    return render_template(
258	        "schedule/staff_calendar.html",
259	        slots=slots, clinicians=clinicians,
260	        week_start=week_start, week_end=week_end,
261	        prev_week=prev_week, next_week=next_week,
262	        selected_clinician=clinician_id,
263	        holiday_dates=holiday_dates,
264	    )
265	
266	
267	# ── Admin: holidays ──
268	@schedule_bp.route("/admin/holidays", methods=["GET", "POST"])
269	@role_required("administrator")
270	@antireplay
271	def holidays():
272	    if request.method == "POST":
273	        holiday_date = request.form.get("date", "").strip()
274	        holiday_name = request.form.get("name", "").strip()
275	        if holiday_date and holiday_name:
276	            try:
277	                d = datetime.strptime(holiday_date, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
278	                existing = Holiday.query.filter_by(date=d).first()
279	                if not existing:
280	                    h = Holiday(date=d, name=holiday_name, created_by=current_user.id)
281	                    db.session.add(h)
282	                    # Block all slots on this date
283	                    Slot.query.filter_by(date=d).update({"status": "holiday"})
284	                    db.session.commit()
285	                    flash("Holiday added.", "success")
286	                else:
287	                    flash("Holiday already exists for this date.", "warning")
288	            except ValueError:
289	                flash("Invalid date.", "danger")
290	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.holidays"))
291	
292	    all_holidays = Holiday.query.order_by(Holiday.date).all()
293	    return render_template("schedule/holidays.html", holidays=all_holidays)
294	
295	
296	@schedule_bp.route("/admin/holidays/<int:holiday_id>/delete", methods=["POST"])
297	@role_required("administrator")
298	@antireplay
299	def delete_holiday(holiday_id):
300	    h = db.session.get(Holiday, holiday_id)
301	    if h:
302	        Slot.query.filter_by(date=h.date, status="holiday").update({"status": "available"})
303	        db.session.delete(h)
304	        db.session.commit()
305	        flash("Holiday removed.", "info")
306	    return redirect(url_for("schedule.holidays"))
307	
308	
309	# ── Admin: bulk generate ──
310	@schedule_bp.route("/admin/bulk-generate", methods=["GET", "POST"])
311	@role_required("administrator")
312	@antireplay
313	def bulk_generate():
314	    clinicians = Clinician.query.all()
315	
316	    rooms = Room.query.filter_by(is_active=True).order_by(Room.name).all()
317	
318	    if request.method == "POST":
319	        clinician_id = request.form.get("clinician_id", type=int)
320	        date_from = request.form.get("date_from", "").strip()
321	        date_to = request.form.get("date_to", "").strip()
322	        room_id = request.form.get("room_id", type=int) or None
323	
324	        if not clinician_id or not date_from or not date_to:
325	            flash("All fields are required.", "danger")
326	            return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
327	
328	        try:
329	            d_from = datetime.strptime(date_from, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
330	            d_to = datetime.strptime(date_to, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
331	        except ValueError:
332	            flash("Invalid dates.", "danger")
333	            return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
334	
335	        templates = ScheduleTemplate.query.filter_by(clinician_id=clinician_id).all()
336	        if not templates:
337	            flash("No schedule template found for this clinician.", "warning")
338	            return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
339	
340	        holiday_dates = {h.date for h in Holiday.query.filter(Holiday.date >= d_from, Holiday.date <= d_to).all()}
341	
342	        created = 0
343	        skipped_conflicts = 0
344	        current = d_from
345	        while current <= d_to:
346	            if current in holiday_dates:
347	                current += timedelta(days=1)
348	                continue
349	
350	            dow = current.weekday()
351	            for tmpl in templates:
352	                if tmpl.day_of_week != dow:
353	                    continue
354	
355	                # Generate slots
356	                t = datetime.combine(current, tmpl.start_time)
357	                end = datetime.combine(current, tmpl.end_time)
358	                while t < end:
359	                    slot_end_time = (t + timedelta(minutes=tmpl.slot_duration)).time()
360	                    existing = Slot.query.filter_by(
361	                        clinician_id=clinician_id, date=current, start_time=t.time()
362	                    ).first()
363	                    if not existing:
364	                        if _has_room_conflict(room_id, current, t.time(), slot_end_time):
365	                            skipped_conflicts += 1
366	                        else:
367	                            slot = Slot(
368	                                clinician_id=clinician_id,
369	                                room_id=room_id,
370	                                date=current,
371	                                start_time=t.time(),
372	                                end_time=slot_end_time,
373	                                capacity=tmpl.capacity,
374	                            )
375	                            db.session.add(slot)
376	                            created += 1
377	                    t += timedelta(minutes=tmpl.slot_duration)
378	
379	            current += timedelta(days=1)
380	
381	        db.session.commit()
382	        msg = f"Generated {created} slots."
383	        if skipped_conflicts:
384	            msg += f" Skipped {skipped_conflicts} slot(s) due to room conflicts."
385	        flash(msg, "success")
386	        return redirect(url_for("schedule.bulk_generate"))
387	
388	    return render_template("schedule/bulk_generate.html", clinicians=clinicians, rooms=rooms)
389

1	import json
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
3	from flask import request, has_request_context
4	from flask_login import current_user
5	from app.extensions import db
6	from app.models.audit import AuditLog, AnomalyAlert
7	from app.models.user import LoginAttempt
8	
9	_NEW_DEVICE_COOLDOWN = timedelta(hours=24)
10	
11	
12	def log_action(action, resource_type, resource_id=None, details=None):
13	    """Log an audit event capturing the current user and request context."""
14	    user_id = None
15	    ip_address = None
16	    user_agent = None
17	
18	    if has_request_context():
19	        if current_user and hasattr(current_user, "id") and current_user.is_authenticated:
20	            user_id = current_user.id
21	        ip_address = request.headers.get("X-Forwarded-For", request.remote_addr) or "unknown"
22	        user_agent = (request.headers.get("User-Agent", "") or "")[:500]
23	
24	    entry = AuditLog(
25	        user_id=user_id,
26	        action=action,
27	        resource_type=resource_type,
28	        resource_id=str(resource_id) if resource_id is not None else None,
29	        details_json=details,
30	        ip_address=ip_address,
31	        user_agent=user_agent,
32	    )
33	    db.session.add(entry)
34	    db.session.commit()
35	    return entry
36	
37	
38	def check_new_device_alert(user_id, username, ip, ua):
39	    """Create AnomalyAlert when a user logs in from a new IP or new device.
40	
41	    A new IP/device is one not seen in any prior successful login for this user.
42	    Alerts are deduplicated within a 24-hour cooldown window per user+IP and
43	    user+device to prevent alert fatigue.  Called after _record_attempt so the
44	    current login is already persisted — we exclude the most-recent record.
45	    """
46	    try:
47	        # All successful logins for this username, oldest-first.
48	        # The last entry is the one just recorded (current login).
49	        all_ok = (
50	            LoginAttempt.query
51	            .filter_by(username=username, success=True)
52	            .order_by(LoginAttempt.attempted_at.asc())
53	            .all()
54	        )
55	        # If this is the very first login there are no prior sessions to compare.
56	        if len(all_ok) <= 1:
57	            return
58	
59	        prior = all_ok[:-1]  # everything before the current login
60	        known_ips = {a.ip_address for a in prior if a.ip_address}
61	        known_uas = {a.user_agent for a in prior if a.user_agent}
62	
63	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
64	        cooldown_start = now - _NEW_DEVICE_COOLDOWN
65	
66	        # ── New IP ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67	        if ip and ip not in known_ips:
68	            already_alerted = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
69	                AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_ip",
70	                AnomalyAlert.created_at >= cooldown_start,
71	                AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
72	                AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"ip={ip}"),
73	            ).first()
74	            if not already_alerted:
75	                db.session.add(AnomalyAlert(
76	                    alert_type="new_ip",
77	                    severity="warning",
78	                    message=f"New IP login: user={username}, ip={ip}",
79	                    details_json=json.dumps({
80	                        "user_id": user_id, "username": username, "ip": ip,
81	                    }),
82	                ))
83	
84	        # ── New device (user-agent) ──────────────────────────────────────────
85	        if ua and ua not in known_uas:
86	            already_alerted = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
87	                AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "new_device",
88	                AnomalyAlert.created_at >= cooldown_start,
89	                AnomalyAlert.message.contains(f"user={username}"),
90	            ).first()
91	            if not already_alerted:
92	                ua_short = ua[:120]
93	                db.session.add(AnomalyAlert(
94	                    alert_type="new_device",
95	                    severity="info",
96	                    message=f"New device login: user={username}, ua={ua_short}",
97	                    details_json=json.dumps({
98	                        "user_id": user_id, "username": username, "user_agent": ua[:200],
99	                    }),
100	                ))
101	
102	        db.session.commit()
103	    except Exception:
104	        db.session.rollback()
105	
106	
107	def anomaly_detection():
108	    """Check for anomalous patterns and create alerts.
109	
110	    Currently checks:
111	    - More than 5 failed login attempts in the last 10 minutes.
112	    """
113	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
114	    window = now - timedelta(minutes=10)
115	
116	    try:
117	        failed_count = LoginAttempt.query.filter(
118	            LoginAttempt.success == False,
119	            LoginAttempt.attempted_at >= window,
120	        ).count()
121	    except Exception:
122	        failed_count = 0
123	
124	    if failed_count > 5:
125	        # Check if we already created an alert for this in the last 10 minutes
126	        recent_alert = AnomalyAlert.query.filter(
127	            AnomalyAlert.alert_type == "failed_logins",
128	            AnomalyAlert.created_at >= window,
129	        ).first()
130	        if not recent_alert:
131	            alert = AnomalyAlert(
132	                alert_type="failed_logins",
133	                severity="warning",
134	                message=f"{failed_count} failed login attempts in the last 10 minutes",
135	            )
136	            db.session.add(alert)
137	            db.session.commit()
138

1	"""Shared test helpers for anti-replay signed request data.
2	
3	The secret must match TestingConfig.REQUEST_SIGNING_SECRET exactly so that the
4	decorator's HMAC verification passes in test runs.
5	"""
6	import hmac
7	import hashlib
8	import uuid
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	
11	TEST_SIGNING_SECRET = "test-request-signing-secret-dev-only"
12	
13	
14	def signed_data(method: str, path: str, extra: dict | None = None) -> dict:
15	    """Return a form-data dict with _nonce, _timestamp, and _signature.
16	
17	    Parameters
18	    ----------
19	    method : HTTP verb (e.g. "POST")
20	    path   : URL path exactly as Flask will see it in request.path
21	             (e.g. "/visits/1/transition")
22	    extra  : additional form fields to merge in (e.g. {"target_state": "checked_in"})
23	    """
24	    nonce = str(uuid.uuid4())
25	    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
26	    payload = f"{method.upper()}|{path}|{nonce}|{timestamp}"
27	    sig = hmac.new(
28	        TEST_SIGNING_SECRET.encode(),
29	        payload.encode(),
30	        hashlib.sha256,
31	    ).hexdigest()
32	    data = {"_nonce": nonce, "_timestamp": timestamp, "_signature": sig}
33	    if extra:
34	        data.update(extra)
35	    return data
36

1	from functools import wraps
2	from flask import abort, request, jsonify
3	from flask_login import current_user, login_required
4	import logging
5	
6	logger = logging.getLogger("meridiancare.auth")
7	
8	
9	def role_required(*roles):
10	    """Decorator that requires the current user to have one of the specified roles."""
11	    def decorator(f):
12	        @wraps(f)
13	        @login_required
14	        def decorated_function(*args, **kwargs):
15	            if current_user.role not in roles:
16	                logger.warning(
17	                    "Access denied: user=%s role=%s required=%s path=%s",
18	                    current_user.username,
19	                    current_user.role,
20	                    roles,
21	                    request.path,
22	                )
23	                if request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
24	                    return jsonify({"error": "Access denied"}), 403
25	                abort(403)
26	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
27	        return decorated_function
28	    return decorator
29

{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\tests\test_on_behalf.py", "content": """"Tests for on-behalf workflows and notes object-level validation.

Covers:
  A) Notes: staff cannot create/read notes for non-patient targets.
  B) On-behalf assessments: front_desk/admin can submit for a patient;
     result.patient_id == patient; unauthorized roles rejected; invalid targets rejected.
  C) On-behalf scheduling: front_desk/admin can hold+confirm for a patient;
     reservation.patient_id == patient; unauthorized roles rejected; invalid targets rejected.
  D) Regression: existing self-service paths unchanged.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, time, timedelta, datetime, timezone
from app.models.user import User
from app.models.assessment import AssessmentResult, AssessmentDraft
from app.models.scheduling import Clinician, Slot, Reservation
from app.models.audit import AuditLog
from app.extensions import db
from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role=role)
        user.set_password(password)
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        return user.id


def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


def _logout(client):
    return client.post("/auth/logout", follow_redirects=True)


def _create_clinician_with_slot(app, username):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
        user.set_password("Password1")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        c = Clinician(user_id=user.id)
        db.session.add(c)
        db.session.commit()
        slot = Slot(
            clinician_id=c.id,
            date=date.today() + timedelta(days=3),
            start_time=time(10, 0),
            end_time=time(10, 30),
            capacity=5,
            status="available",
        )
        db.session.add(slot)
        db.session.commit()
        return c.id, slot.id


LOW_RISK_ANSWERS = {
    **{f"phq9_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 10)},
    **{f"gad7_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 8)},
    "bp_category": "Normal",
    "fall_history": "no", "mobility_aids": "no", "dizziness": "no", "balance_meds": "no",
    "med_adherence": "never_miss",
}


def _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, token):
    """Walk a full behalf assessment wizard and submit."""
    client.get(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/start")
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = token
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/step/{step}", data=all_data)
    path = f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/submit"
    return client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# A) Notes object-level validation
# ===========================================================================

def test_notes_rejects_non_patient_target(client, app):
    """Staff must get an error when trying to view/create notes for a non-patient user."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_notes_staff", role="front_desk")
    clinician_id = _create_user(app, "ob_notes_clinician", role="clinician")

    _login(client, "ob_notes_staff")
    path = f"/notes/patient/{clinician_id}"
    # GET request — should redirect away (not render notes)
    resp = client.get(path, follow_redirects=True)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"not a patient" in resp.data.lower() or b"patient not found" in resp.data.lower() or b"patient list" in resp.data.lower()


def test_notes_rejects_non_patient_target_post(client, app):
    """POST to notes for a non-patient target must be rejected."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_notes_staff2", role="administrator")
    fd_id = _create_user(app, "ob_notes_fd_target", role="front_desk")

    _login(client, "ob_notes_staff2")
    path = f"/notes/patient/{fd_id}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "some note"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    # No note should have been created
    with app.app_context():
        from app.models.clinical_note import ClinicalNote
        notes = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=fd_id).all()
        assert len(notes) == 0


def test_notes_accepts_patient_target(client, app):
    """Staff can create notes for a patient-role user — sanity check."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_notes_staff3", role="clinician")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_notes_patient3", role="patient")

    _login(client, "ob_notes_staff3")
    path = f"/notes/patient/{patient_id}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Routine checkup note"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    with app.app_context():
        from app.models.clinical_note import ClinicalNote
        notes = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=patient_id).all()
        assert len(notes) == 1


# ===========================================================================
# B) On-behalf assessments
# ===========================================================================

def test_front_desk_can_submit_behalf_assessment(client, app):
    """front_desk can complete and submit an assessment for a patient."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_fd_staff", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_fd_patient")

    _login(client, "ob_fd_staff")
    resp = _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, "behalf-tok-fd")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_admin_can_submit_behalf_assessment(client, app):
    """administrator can complete and submit an assessment for a patient."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_admin_staff", role="administrator")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_admin_patient")

    _login(client, "ob_admin_staff")
    resp = _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, "behalf-tok-admin")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_behalf_assessment_result_owned_by_patient(client, app):
    """AssessmentResult.patient_id must equal the target patient, not the staff user."""
    staff_id = _create_user(app, "ob_own_staff", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_own_patient")

    _login(client, "ob_own_staff")
    _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, "behalf-tok-own")

    with app.app_context():
        result = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(patient_id=patient_id).first()
        assert result is not None, "AssessmentResult was not created"
        assert result.patient_id == patient_id
        assert result.patient_id != staff_id


def test_behalf_assessment_audit_log_created(client, app):
    """An audit log entry must be written for on-behalf assessment submission."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_audit_staff", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_audit_patient")

    _login(client, "ob_audit_staff")
    _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, "behalf-tok-audit")

    with app.app_context():
        entry = AuditLog.query.filter_by(action="on_behalf_assessment").first()
        assert entry is not None
        import json
        details = json.loads(entry.details_json)
        assert details["patient_id"] == patient_id


def test_behalf_assessment_idempotency(client, app):
    """Double-submit with the same token must not create a duplicate result."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_idem_staff", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_idem_patient")

    _login(client, "ob_idem_staff")
    # First submission — creates draft and result
    _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, "behalf-tok-idem")
    # Second submission — same token, should be idempotent
    # Need to restart wizard so a fresh draft exists
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = "behalf-tok-idem"
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/step/{step}", data=all_data)
    path = f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/submit"
    client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": "behalf-tok-idem"}), follow_redirects=True)

    with app.app_context():
        import hashlib
        token_hash = hashlib.sha256("behalf-tok-idem".encode()).hexdigest()
        count = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(request_token=token_hash).count()
        assert count == 1


def test_unauthorized_role_cannot_use_behalf_assessment(client, app):
    """A clinician (not front_desk or admin) must be denied access to behalf routes."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_deny_clin", role="clinician")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_deny_patient")

    _login(client, "ob_deny_clin")
    resp = client.get(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/start", follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code in (302, 403)


def test_patient_cannot_use_behalf_assessment(client, app):
    """A patient must be denied access to behalf assessment routes."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_pat_self")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_pat_target")

    _login(client, "ob_pat_self")
    resp = client.get(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/start", follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code in (302, 403)


def test_behalf_assessment_rejects_nonexistent_patient(client, app):
    """behalf_start must redirect/error when patient_id does not exist."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_noexist_staff", role="front_desk")

    _login(client, "ob_noexist_staff")
    resp = client.get("/assessments/behalf/99999/start", follow_redirects=True)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"not found" in resp.data.lower() or b"patient" in resp.data.lower()


def test_behalf_assessment_rejects_non_patient_target(client, app):
    """behalf routes must reject targets who are not patient-role users."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_nonpat_staff", role="front_desk")
    admin_id = _create_user(app, "ob_nonpat_admin_target", role="administrator")

    _login(client, "ob_nonpat_staff")
    resp = client.get(f"/assessments/behalf/{admin_id}/start", follow_redirects=True)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"not a patient" in resp.data.lower()


# ===========================================================================
# C) On-behalf scheduling
# ===========================================================================

def test_front_desk_can_hold_slot_for_patient(client, app):
    """front_desk can create a hold on behalf of a patient."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_sched_doc1")
    _create_user(app, "ob_sched_fd1", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat1")

    _login(client, "ob_sched_fd1")
    path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/hold/{slot_id}"
    resp = client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path), follow_redirects=False)
    # Should redirect to behalf confirm page
    assert resp.status_code == 302


def test_behalf_hold_reservation_owned_by_patient(client, app):
    """Reservation.patient_id must equal the target patient, not the staff user."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_sched_doc2")
    staff_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_fd2", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat2")

    _login(client, "ob_sched_fd2")
    path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/hold/{slot_id}"
    client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path), follow_redirects=False)

    with app.app_context():
        res = Reservation.query.filter_by(patient_id=patient_id).first()
        assert res is not None, "Reservation was not created"
        assert res.patient_id == patient_id
        assert res.patient_id != staff_id


def test_behalf_hold_audit_log_created(client, app):
    """An audit log entry must be written for on-behalf hold."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_sched_doc3")
    _create_user(app, "ob_sched_fd3", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat3")

    _login(client, "ob_sched_fd3")
    path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/hold/{slot_id}"
    client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path), follow_redirects=False)

    with app.app_context():
        entry = AuditLog.query.filter_by(action="on_behalf_hold").first()
        assert entry is not None
        import json
        details = json.loads(entry.details_json)
        assert details["patient_id"] == patient_id
        assert details["slot_id"] == slot_id


def test_behalf_confirm_completes_booking(client, app):
    """Staff can confirm a held reservation on behalf of a patient."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_sched_doc4")
    _create_user(app, "ob_sched_fd4", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat4")

    _login(client, "ob_sched_fd4")

    # Hold
    hold_path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/hold/{slot_id}"
    resp = client.post(hold_path, data=signed_data("POST", hold_path), follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code == 302
    location = resp.headers.get("Location", "")
    assert f"/behalf/{patient_id}/confirm/" in location

    # Extract reservation id from redirect
    res_id = int(location.rstrip("/").split("/")[-1])

    # Confirm
    confirm_path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/confirm/{res_id}"
    resp = client.post(confirm_path, data=signed_data("POST", confirm_path), follow_redirects=True)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        res = db.session.get(Reservation, res_id)
        assert res.status == "confirmed"
        assert res.patient_id == patient_id


def test_behalf_confirm_audit_log_created(client, app):
    """An audit log entry must be written for on-behalf confirm."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_sched_doc5")
    _create_user(app, "ob_sched_fd5", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat5")

{"file_path": "C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\claudeTask\Task-2\repo\tests\test_on_behalf.py", "content": """"Tests for on-behalf workflows and notes object-level validation.

Covers:
  A) Notes: staff cannot create/read notes for non-patient targets.
  B) On-behalf assessments: front_desk/admin can submit for a patient;
     result.patient_id == patient; unauthorized roles rejected; invalid targets rejected.
  C) On-behalf scheduling: front_desk/admin can hold+confirm for a patient;
     reservation.patient_id == patient; unauthorized roles rejected; invalid targets rejected.
  D) Regression: existing self-service paths unchanged.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, time, timedelta, datetime, timezone
from app.models.user import User
from app.models.assessment import AssessmentResult, AssessmentDraft
from app.models.scheduling import Clinician, Slot, Reservation
from app.models.audit import AuditLog
from app.extensions import db
from tests.signing_helpers import signed_data


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _create_user(app, username, role="patient", password="Password1"):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role=role)
        user.set_password(password)
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        return user.id


def _login(client, username, password="Password1"):
    return client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        data={"username": username, "password": password},
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


def _logout(client):
    return client.post("/auth/logout", follow_redirects=True)


def _create_clinician_with_slot(app, username):
    with app.app_context():
        user = User(username=username, role="clinician")
        user.set_password("Password1")
        db.session.add(user)
        db.session.commit()
        c = Clinician(user_id=user.id)
        db.session.add(c)
        db.session.commit()
        slot = Slot(
            clinician_id=c.id,
            date=date.today() + timedelta(days=3),
            start_time=time(10, 0),
            end_time=time(10, 30),
            capacity=5,
            status="available",
        )
        db.session.add(slot)
        db.session.commit()
        return c.id, slot.id


LOW_RISK_ANSWERS = {
    **{f"phq9_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 10)},
    **{f"gad7_q{i}": "0" for i in range(1, 8)},
    "bp_category": "Normal",
    "fall_history": "no", "mobility_aids": "no", "dizziness": "no", "balance_meds": "no",
    "med_adherence": "never_miss",
}


def _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, token):
    """Walk a full behalf assessment wizard and submit."""
    client.get(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/start")
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = token
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/step/{step}", data=all_data)
    path = f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/submit"
    return client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": token}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# A) Notes object-level validation
# ===========================================================================

def test_notes_rejects_non_patient_target(client, app):
    """Staff must get an error when trying to view/create notes for a non-patient user."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_notes_staff", role="front_desk")
    clinician_id = _create_user(app, "ob_notes_clinician", role="clinician")

    _login(client, "ob_notes_staff")
    path = f"/notes/patient/{clinician_id}"
    # GET request — should redirect away (not render notes)
    resp = client.get(path, follow_redirects=True)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert b"not a patient" in resp.data.lower() or b"patient not found" in resp.data.lower() or b"patient list" in resp.data.lower()


def test_notes_rejects_non_patient_target_post(client, app):
    """POST to notes for a non-patient target must be rejected."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_notes_staff2", role="administrator")
    fd_id = _create_user(app, "ob_notes_fd_target", role="front_desk")

    _login(client, "ob_notes_staff2")
    path = f"/notes/patient/{fd_id}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "some note"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    # No note should have been created
    with app.app_context():
        from app.models.clinical_note import ClinicalNote
        notes = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=fd_id).all()
        assert len(notes) == 0


def test_notes_accepts_patient_target(client, app):
    """Staff can create notes for a patient-role user — sanity check."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_notes_staff3", role="clinician")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_notes_patient3", role="patient")

    _login(client, "ob_notes_staff3")
    path = f"/notes/patient/{patient_id}"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"content": "Routine checkup note"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    with app.app_context():
        from app.models.clinical_note import ClinicalNote
        notes = ClinicalNote.query.filter_by(patient_id=patient_id).all()
        assert len(notes) == 1

        import json

        assert details["patient_id"] == patient_id


def test_behalf_assessment_idempotency(client, app):
    """Double-submit with the same token must not create a duplicate result."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_idem_staff", role="front_desk")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_idem_patient")

    _login(client, "ob_idem_staff")
    # First submission — creates draft and result
    _behalf_submit_assessment(client, patient_id, "behalf-tok-idem")
    # Second submission — same token, should be idempotent
    # Need to restart wizard so a fresh draft exists
    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = "behalf-tok-idem"
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/step/{step}", data=all_data)
    path = f"/assessments/behalf/{patient_id}/submit"
    client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": "behalf-tok-idem"}), follow_redirects=True)

    with app.app_context():
        import hashlib
        token_hash = hashlib.sha256("behalf-tok-idem".encode()).hexdigest()
        count = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(request_token=token_hash).count()
        assert count == 1

def test_patient_cannot_behalf_hold(client, app):
    """A patient must be denied access to behalf scheduling routes."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_sched_doc10")
    _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat10a")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat10b")

    _login(client, "ob_sched_pat10a")
    path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/hold/{slot_id}"
    resp = client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path), follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code in (302, 403)


def test_admin_can_behalf_hold_and_confirm(client, app):
    """administrator role can also use behalf scheduling routes."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_sched_doc11")
    _create_user(app, "ob_sched_admin11", role="administrator")
    patient_id = _create_user(app, "ob_sched_pat11")

    _login(client, "ob_sched_admin11")
    hold_path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/hold/{slot_id}"
    resp = client.post(hold_path, data=signed_data("POST", hold_path), follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code == 302

    res_id = int(resp.headers["Location"].rstrip("/").split("/")[-1])
    confirm_path = f"/schedule/behalf/{patient_id}/confirm/{res_id}"
    resp = client.post(confirm_path, data=signed_data("POST", confirm_path), follow_redirects=True)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        res = db.session.get(Reservation, res_id)
        assert res.status == "confirmed"
        assert res.patient_id == patient_id


# ===========================================================================
# D) Regression: self-service paths unchanged
# ===========================================================================

def test_patient_self_assessment_unaffected(client, app):
    """Regular patient-initiated assessment still works after on-behalf additions."""
    _create_user(app, "ob_reg_patient")
    _login(client, "ob_reg_patient")
    client.get("/assessments/start")

    all_data = dict(LOW_RISK_ANSWERS)
    all_data["request_token"] = "ob-reg-tok"
    for step in range(1, 6):
        client.post(f"/assessments/step/{step}", data=all_data)

    path = "/assessments/submit"
    resp = client.post(
        path,
        data=signed_data("POST", path, {"request_token": "ob-reg-tok"}),
        follow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    with app.app_context():
        patient = User.query.filter_by(username="ob_reg_patient").first()
        results = AssessmentResult.query.filter_by(patient_id=patient.id).all()
        assert len(results) == 1
        assert results[0].risk_level == "Low"


def test_patient_self_booking_unaffected(client, app):
    """Regular patient-initiated hold still works after on-behalf additions."""
    cid, slot_id = _create_clinician_with_slot(app, "ob_reg_doc")
    _create_user(app, "ob_reg_pat")

    _login(client, "ob_reg_pat")
    path = f"/schedule/hold/{slot_id}"
    resp = client.post(path, data=signed_data("POST", path), follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code == 302
    assert "/confirm/" in resp.headers.get("Location", "")

    with app.app_context():
        patient = User.query.filter_by(username="ob_reg_pat").first()
        res = Reservation.query.filter_by(patient_id=patient.id).first()
        assert res is not None
        assert res.status == "held"

## Summary

### Files changed

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `app/routes/notes.py` | Added `patient.role != "patient"` check — rejects non-patient targets on both GET and POST with a flash and redirect |
| `app/routes/assessments.py` | Added `_get_behalf_patient()` helper + three new routes: `behalf_start`, `behalf_step`, `behalf_submit` — full wizard flow for staff acting on behalf; result owned by target patient; audit log written on submit; anti-replay + token hashing preserved |
| `app/routes/schedule.py` | Added `_get_behalf_schedule_patient()` helper + three new routes: `behalf_hold`, `behalf_confirm_page`, `behalf_confirm` — hold/confirm flow for staff acting on behalf; reservation owned by target patient; audit log written on hold and confirm; all hold expiry / capacity / conflict protections retained |
| `app/templates/assessments/behalf_wizard.html` | New wizard shell showing "Acting on behalf of" banner |
| `app/templates/assessments/_behalf_step.html` | Wizard step partial posting to `behalf_step` URL |
| `app/templates/assessments/_behalf_review.html` | Review partial posting to `behalf_submit` URL with anti-replay inputs |
| `app/templates/schedule/behalf_confirm.html` | Confirm page posting to `behalf_confirm` URL with anti-replay inputs |
| `tests/test_on_behalf.py` | 25 new tests covering all acceptance criteria: notes non-patient rejection, behalf assessment ownership + audit + idempotency + role/target guards, behalf scheduling ownership + audit + role/target guards, and regression checks for existing self-service flows |

### Remaining limitations

None — all existing tests continue to pass, and the new paths use the same security primitives (CSRF, anti-replay, token hashing, role decorators) as the existing routes.
